zh.result: 在多个数据集上超越现有方法，保持实时推理速度。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种融合相机与激光雷达进行3D占用预测的高效方案，尤其适用于自动驾驶场景理解。

idea_spark.idea: 利用LiDAR高度先验动态调整投影空间，使采样点集中在几何有意义区域。
idea_spark.risk: LiDAR高度噪声可能引入训练不稳定，渐进式条件策略可缓解但需额外调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将稀疏点云的高度信息显式编码为BEV图，指导2D-3D特征映射，启发其他多模态融合方法。

en.tldr: Proposes HiPR, which uses LiDAR height priors to adaptively reparameterize the projection space, improving camera-LiDAR 3D occupancy prediction.
en.motivation: Existing methods uniformly sample in a fixed projection space, failing to capture scene sparsity and height variations, leading to ambiguous correspondences.
en.method: Encodes LiDAR into a BEV height map, adjusts each pillar's sampling range accordingly for adaptive reparameterization, masks invalid height regions, and introduces progressive height conditioning during training.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on multiple datasets while maintaining real-time inference.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient fusion approach for camera-LiDAR 3D occupancy prediction, especially useful for autonomous driving scene understanding.

idea_spark.idea: Dynamically adjust projection space using LiDAR height priors to concentrate sampling on geometrically meaningful regions.
idea_spark.risk: LiDAR height noise may cause training instability; progressive conditioning mitigates this but requires extra tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explicitly encoding sparse point cloud heights as a BEV map to guide 2D-to-3D feature mapping inspires other multi-modal fusion methods.

papers.title: Implicit Representations of Grammaticality in Language Models
papers.summary: Grammaticality and likelihood are distinct notions in human language. Pretrained language models (LMs), which are probabilistic models of language fitted to maximize corpus likelihood, generate grammatically well-formed text and discriminate well between grammatical and ungrammatical sentences in tightly controlled minimal pairs. However, their string probabilities do not sharply discriminate between grammatical and ungrammatical sentences overall. But do LMs implicitly acquire a grammaticality distinction distinct from string probability? We explore this question through studying internal representations of LMs, by training a linear probe on a dataset of grammatical and (synthetic) ungrammatical sentences obtained by applying perturbations to a naturalistic text corpus. We find that this simple grammaticality probe generalizes to human-curated grammaticality judgment benchmarks and outperforms LM probability-based grammaticality judgments. When applied to semantic plausibility benchmarks, in which both members of a minimal pair are grammatical and differ in only plausibility, the probe however performs worse than string probability. The English-trained probe also exhibits nontrivial cross-lingual generalization, outperforming string probabilities on grammaticality benchmarks in numerous other languages. Additionally, probe scores correlate only weakly with string probabilities. These results collectively suggest that LMs acquire to some extent an implicit grammaticality distinction within their hidden layers.

ai.tldr: 预训练语言模型在内部表示中隐式编码了语法性，这种表示与字符串概率不同，且能跨语言泛化。
ai.motivation: 人类语言中语法性和似然性是不同概念，但语言模型作为概率模型，其字符串概率不能很好区分语法性，因此探究模型内部是否隐式存在语法性表示。
ai.method: 在自然语料上构造语法和（合成）非语法句子数据集，训练线性探针，并在人工语法性判断基准和语义合理性基准上评估。
ai.result: 语法性探针在语法性判断上优于基于概率的方法，但在语义合理性上较差；探针分数与字符串概率弱相关，且能跨语言泛化。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解语言模型如何隐式学习语法知识，以及如何通过探针提取这种知识。

idea_spark.idea: 利用探针分数作为特征，改进语法错误检测或语言生成中的语法控制。
idea_spark.risk: 探针可能依赖特定扰动模式，泛化到真实语法错误时效果未知。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将语法性作为独立于概率的表示，启发设计更符合人类语言能力的评估指标。

zh.tldr: 预训练语言模型在内部表示中隐式编码了语法性，这种表示与字符串概率不同，且能跨语言泛化。
zh.motivation: 人类语言中语法性和似然性是不同概念，但语言模型作为概率模型，其字符串概率不能很好区分语法性，因此探究模型内部是否隐式存在语法性表示。
zh.method: 在自然语料上构造语法和（合成）非语法句子数据集，训练线性探针，并在人工语法性判断基准和语义合理性基准上评估。
zh.result: 语法性探针在语法性判断上优于基于概率的方法，但在语义合理性上较差；探针分数与字符串概率弱相关，且能跨语言泛化。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解语言模型如何隐式学习语法知识，以及如何通过探针提取这种知识。

idea_spark.idea: 利用探针分数作为特征，改进语法错误检测或语言生成中的语法控制。
idea_spark.risk: 探针可能依赖特定扰动模式，泛化到真实语法错误时效果未知。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将语法性作为独立于概率的表示，启发设计更符合人类语言能力的评估指标。

en.tldr: Pretrained language models implicitly encode grammaticality in their internal representations, distinct from string probability, and this representation generalizes cross-lingually.
en.motivation: Grammaticality and likelihood are distinct in human language; LMs' string probabilities poorly discriminate grammaticality, so we investigate whether LMs have an implicit grammaticality distinction internally.
en.method: Train a linear probe on a dataset of grammatical and synthetic ungrammatical sentences derived from natural text, then evaluate on human-curated grammaticality and semantic plausibility benchmarks.
en.result: The grammaticality probe outperforms probability-based judgments on grammaticality, but underperforms on plausibility; probe scores weakly correlate with string probabilities and generalize cross-lingually.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how LMs implicitly learn grammatical knowledge and how to extract it via probing.

idea_spark.idea: Use probe scores as features for grammar error detection or controlled text generation.
idea_spark.risk: Probe may rely on specific perturbation patterns, limiting generalization to real-world ungrammaticality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Treating grammaticality as a representation independent of probability inspires new evaluation metrics aligned with human language competence.

papers.title: Communication Offloading on SmartNIC DPUs: A Quantitative Approach
papers.summary: SmartNIC Data Processing Units (DPUs) offer a promising solution for saving high-end CPU resources by offloading tasks to programmable cores near the network interface. In this work, we explore the feasibility of SmartNIC DPUs in supporting an asynchronous communication model called "fire-and-forget", particularly its core message routing service. We design a communication offloading engine called Buddy that decouples communication tasks from the application process. Buddy runs flexibly on SmartNIC DPUs such as the Nvidia BlueField-3 DPU and generic x86 CPUs. Our evaluation results in five applications identify the memory-to-communication ratio as a key predictor of the offloading performance. Host-dominated workloads, such as Quicksilver and Sparse Matrix Transpose, achieved up to 1.55x speedup with communication offloaded to the DPU. We further identify a 625x increase in DRAM traffic due to the absence of Direct Cache Access support on the DPU, highlighting a critical need in future SmartNIC designs.

ai.tldr: 本文探索了在SmartNIC DPU上卸载异步通信任务（fire-and-forget）的可行性，设计了Buddy引擎，并发现内存-通信比是性能关键预测因子，DPU上缺乏直接缓存访问导致DRAM流量增加625倍。
ai.motivation: 节省高端CPU资源，利用SmartNIC DPU的可编程核心卸载通信任务。
ai.method: 设计Buddy通信卸载引擎，在Nvidia BlueField-3 DPU和通用x86 CPU上运行，通过解耦通信任务与应用程序进程实现灵活卸载。
ai.result: 在Quicksilver和稀疏矩阵转置等主机主导负载中，卸载到DPU获得最高1.55倍加速；DPU上DRAM流量因缺乏直接缓存访问增加625倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解SmartNIC DPU在通信卸载中的性能特征和瓶颈，指导未来SmartNIC设计。

idea_spark.idea: 设计缓存一致性协议或预取机制来缓解DPU上DRAM流量激增问题。
idea_spark.risk: 硬件修改成本高，且可能引入新的延迟或复杂性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从网络接口卸载通信的思路可启发在边缘设备上卸载部分网络协议处理。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解SmartNIC DPU在通信卸载中的性能特征和瓶颈，指导未来SmartNIC设计。

idea_spark.idea: 设计缓存一致性协议或预取机制来缓解DPU上DRAM流量激增问题。
idea_spark.risk: 硬件修改成本高，且可能引入新的延迟或复杂性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从网络接口卸载通信的思路可启发在边缘设备上卸载部分网络协议处理。

en.tldr: This paper explores the feasibility of offloading asynchronous communication (fire-and-forget) onto SmartNIC DPUs, designs the Buddy engine, and identifies the memory-to-communication ratio as a key performance predictor, with a 625x increase in DRAM traffic due to lack of Direct Cache Access on the DPU.
en.motivation: Save high-end CPU resources by offloading communication tasks to programmable cores on SmartNIC DPUs.
en.method: Design the Buddy communication offloading engine running on Nvidia BlueField-3 DPU and generic x86 CPUs, decoupling communication tasks from application processes.
en.result: Up to 1.55x speedup on host-dominated workloads like Quicksilver and Sparse Matrix Transpose; 625x increase in DRAM traffic on DPU due to lack of Direct Cache Access.
en.help_to_user: Understand performance characteristics and bottlenecks of SmartNIC DPU communication offloading, guiding future SmartNIC designs.

idea_spark.idea: Design cache coherence protocols or prefetching mechanisms to mitigate DRAM traffic surge on DPUs.
idea_spark.risk: Hardware modifications are costly and may introduce new latency or complexity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Offloading communication from the network interface inspires offloading parts of network protocol processing on edge devices.

papers.title: CTseg: A Tool for Brain CT Segmentation, Spatial Normalisation, and Volumetrics
papers.summary: This paper presents and validates CTseg, a freely available software for brain CT segmentation, spatial normalisation, and volumetrics. CTseg builds on the Multi-Brain generative modelling framework, providing a CT-specific pipeline that produces tissue maps, deformation fields, and brain volume estimates in the same format as SPM's unified segmentation, thereby extending SPM's established analysis chain from MRI to CT. CTseg is designed for routine hospital CT scans without requiring preprocessing or resampling in deployment. Although CTseg has been adopted in clinical research spanning, among other things, stroke, dementia, and brain morphometry, a systematic validation against an independent reference standard has been lacking. Using paired MR/CT head scans, we evaluate CTseg across four dimensions: segmentation accuracy against an MRI-derived silver standard; spatial normalisation consistency through group-average sharpness and voxelwise coefficient of variation; brain volume agreement via intraclass correlation and Bland-Altman analysis; and downstream sex classification performance from normalised tissue maps. As a baseline, we apply SPM's MRI-based unified segmentation directly to the CT images. CTseg significantly outperformed this baseline for segmentation and normalisation, showed stronger TBV agreement, and achieved comparable TIV agreement. CTseg is freely available at https://github.com/WCHN/CTseg, and all experiment code is included in the repository for full reproducibility.

ai.tldr: CTseg是一个免费软件，用于脑CT分割、空间归一化和体积测量，基于Multi-Brain生成建模框架，输出与SPM统一分割格式一致的组织图、变形场和脑体积估计，无需预处理即可用于常规医院CT扫描。
ai.motivation: 现有SPM分析链主要针对MRI，缺乏适用于常规CT的标准化分割和归一化工具，且CTseg虽已用于临床研究但缺乏独立参考标准的系统验证。
ai.method: 基于Multi-Brain生成建模框架构建CT专用管道，输出组织图、变形场和体积估计；使用配对MR/CT头部扫描，从分割精度、空间归一化一致性、脑体积一致性和下游性别分类四个维度评估。
ai.result: CTseg在分割和归一化上显著优于直接对CT应用SPM的MRI统一分割基线，在总脑体积一致性上更强，总颅内体积一致性相当。
ai.help_to_user: 提供可直接用于临床CT的脑分割和体积测量工具，无需预处理，便于研究者快速获得标准化结果。

idea_spark.idea: 将CTseg与深度学习结合，开发端到端的分割和归一化网络，进一步提升速度和精度。
idea_spark.risk: 生成模型对异常解剖（如大肿瘤）可能泛化不足，需额外验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用配对MR/CT数据系统验证CT分割方法，为跨模态分析提供可靠基准。

zh.tldr: CTseg是一个免费软件，用于脑CT分割、空间归一化和体积测量，基于Multi-Brain生成建模框架，输出与SPM统一分割格式一致的组织图、变形场和脑体积估计，无需预处理即可用于常规医院CT扫描。

zh.result: CTseg在分割和归一化上显著优于直接对CT应用SPM的MRI统一分割基线，在总脑体积一致性上更强，总颅内体积一致性相当。

idea_spark.idea: 将CTseg与深度学习结合，开发端到端的分割和归一化网络，进一步提升速度和精度。
idea_spark.risk: 生成模型对异常解剖（如大肿瘤）可能泛化不足，需额外验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用配对MR/CT数据系统验证CT分割方法，为跨模态分析提供可靠基准。

en.tldr: CTseg is a free software for brain CT segmentation, spatial normalisation, and volumetrics, built on the Multi-Brain generative modelling framework, producing tissue maps, deformation fields, and brain volume estimates in SPM-compatible format without preprocessing for routine hospital CT scans.
en.motivation: Existing SPM analysis chain is MRI-oriented, lacking a standardised CT segmentation and normalisation tool for routine scans; CTseg has been used clinically but lacked systematic validation against an independent reference standard.
en.method: Built on the Multi-Brain generative modelling framework to produce tissue maps, deformation fields, and volume estimates; evaluated on paired MR/CT head scans across segmentation accuracy, spatial normalisation consistency, brain volume agreement, and downstream sex classification.
en.result: CTseg significantly outperformed the baseline (direct SPM MRI-based segmentation on CT) in segmentation and normalisation, showed stronger TBV agreement, and comparable TIV agreement.
en.help_to_user: Provides a ready-to-use tool for CT brain segmentation and volumetrics without preprocessing, enabling researchers to obtain standardised results quickly.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate CTseg with deep learning to develop an end-to-end segmentation and normalisation network for improved speed and accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Generative models may generalise poorly to abnormal anatomy (e.g., large tumours), requiring additional validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Systematic validation using paired MR/CT data provides a reliable benchmark for cross-modal CT segmentation methods.

papers.title: Chaotic Contrastive Learning for Robust Texture Classification
papers.summary: Texture classification is a pivotal task in computer vision, presenting unique challenges due to high inter-class similarity and the sensitivity of structural patterns to scale and illumination changes. While Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and recent Vision Transformers have set performance benchmarks, they often require extensive labeled datasets or struggle to generalize across domains due to an over-reliance on color and shape features. This paper introduces a novel framework that synergizes Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) with deterministic chaotic dynamics. We propose a chaotic contrastive pre-training strategy, where pixel-wise chaotic maps, specifically Logistic, Tent, and Sine maps, act as non-linear data augmentation techniques. These chaotic perturbations, grounded in ergodic theory, force the network to learn topologically robust features by mimicking complex environmental noise and reflectance variations. Furthermore, we introduce an attention-based feature ensemble that fuses high-level semantic representations from a supervised large backbone with low-frequency structural features from a chaos-pretrained tiny encoder. Experimental results on six texture benchmarks (FMD, UMD, KTH-TIPS2-b, DTD, GTOS, and 1200Tex) demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method, outperforming state-of-the-art approaches and achieving promising accuracies on all the analyzed datasets.

ai.tldr: 提出混沌对比学习框架，结合自监督学习与确定性混沌动力学，通过混沌映射增强数据，提升纹理分类的鲁棒性。
ai.motivation: 纹理分类面临类间相似度高、对尺度和光照敏感等挑战，现有方法依赖大量标注数据或过度关注颜色形状特征，泛化能力不足。
ai.method: 采用Logistic、Tent和Sine混沌映射作为非线性数据增强，基于遍历理论迫使网络学习拓扑鲁棒特征；并设计注意力特征集成，融合大骨干网络的语义特征与混沌预训练小编码器的低频结构特征。
ai.result: 在六个纹理基准数据集上超越现有方法，取得最优准确率。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需大量标注数据即可提升纹理分类鲁棒性的新思路，尤其适用于环境噪声和光照变化大的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索不同混沌映射的组合或自适应选择策略，或结合其他自监督学习范式（如掩码图像建模）进一步强化特征学习。
idea_spark.risk: 混沌映射的随机性可能导致训练不稳定，需仔细调参；对特定纹理数据集可能过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将非线性动力学系统引入数据增强，启发利用更丰富的物理或数学变换来模拟真实世界噪声和变化。

zh.tldr: 提出混沌对比学习框架，结合自监督学习与确定性混沌动力学，通过混沌映射增强数据，提升纹理分类的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索不同混沌映射的组合或自适应选择策略，或结合其他自监督学习范式（如掩码图像建模）进一步强化特征学习。
idea_spark.risk: 混沌映射的随机性可能导致训练不稳定，需仔细调参；对特定纹理数据集可能过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将非线性动力学系统引入数据增强，启发利用更丰富的物理或数学变换来模拟真实世界噪声和变化。

en.tldr: Proposes a chaotic contrastive learning framework that integrates self-supervised learning with deterministic chaotic dynamics, using chaotic maps for data augmentation to achieve robust texture classification.
en.motivation: Texture classification suffers from high inter-class similarity and sensitivity to scale and illumination; existing methods require extensive labeled data or over-rely on color/shape features, limiting generalization.
en.method: Employs Logistic, Tent, and Sine chaotic maps as nonlinear data augmentation, grounded in ergodic theory to force learning of topologically robust features; introduces an attention-based feature ensemble fusing high-level semantics from a large backbone with low-frequency structural features from a chaos-pretrained tiny encoder.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on six texture benchmarks (FMD, UMD, KTH-TIPS2-b, DTD, GTOS, 1200Tex), achieving top accuracies.
en.help_to_user: Offers a label-efficient approach to robust texture classification, particularly beneficial in environments with noise and illumination variations.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combinations or adaptive selection of chaotic maps, or integrate with other self-supervised paradigms like masked image modeling for enhanced feature learning.
idea_spark.risk: Stochasticity of chaotic maps may cause training instability; careful hyperparameter tuning needed; potential overfitting on specific texture datasets.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introducing nonlinear dynamical systems into data augmentation inspires using richer physical or mathematical transforms to simulate real-world noise and variations.

papers.title: ULF-Loc: Unbiased Landmark Feature for Robust Visual Localization with 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.summary: Visual localization is a core technology for augmented reality and autonomous navigation. Recent methods combine the efficient rendering of 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) with feature-based localization. These methods rely on direct matching between 2D query features and the 3D Gaussian feature field, but this often results in mismatches due to an inherent bias in the learned Gaussian feature. We theoretically analyze the feature learning process in 3DGS, revealing that the widely adopted $α$-blending optimization inherently introduces bias into 3D point features. This bias stems from the entanglement between individual Gaussians and their neighboring Gaussians, making the learned features unsuitable for precise matching tasks. Motivated by these findings, we propose ULF-Loc, an unbiased landmark feature framework that replaces biased feature optimization with geometry-weighted feature fusion. We further introduce keypoint-consensus landmark sampling to select reliable Gaussians and local geometric consistency verification to reject mismatches caused by rendering artifacts. On the Cambridge Landmarks dataset, ULF-Loc reduces the mean median translation error by 17\% compared to the state-of-the-art, while achieving superior efficiency with only 1/10 the training time and 1/6 the GPU memory of STDLoc.

ai.tldr: 提出ULF-Loc框架，通过无偏地标特征和几何一致性验证，在3D高斯泼溅中实现鲁棒视觉定位，显著降低误差并提升效率。
ai.motivation: 现有基于3DGS的定位方法因α混合优化引入特征偏差，导致2D-3D匹配不准确。
ai.method: 用几何加权特征融合替代有偏特征优化，引入关键点共识地标采样和局部几何一致性验证。
ai.result: 在Cambridge Landmarks数据集上，平均中位数平移误差降低17%，训练时间仅1/10，GPU内存仅1/6。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、鲁棒的视觉定位方案，适用于增强现实和自主导航。

idea_spark.idea: 将几何先验（如深度、法线）显式融入特征融合，进一步消除渲染伪影导致的误匹配。
idea_spark.risk: 几何加权可能对噪声敏感，需验证在极端光照或动态场景下的稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从特征偏差的理论分析出发，启发其他隐式表示（如NeRF）中特征解耦的研究。

zh.tldr: 提出ULF-Loc框架，通过无偏地标特征和几何一致性验证，在3D高斯泼溅中实现鲁棒视觉定位，显著降低误差并提升效率。
zh.motivation: 现有基于3DGS的定位方法因α混合优化引入特征偏差，导致2D-3D匹配不准确。
zh.method: 用几何加权特征融合替代有偏特征优化，引入关键点共识地标采样和局部几何一致性验证。
zh.result: 在Cambridge Landmarks数据集上，平均中位数平移误差降低17%，训练时间仅1/10，GPU内存仅1/6。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、鲁棒的视觉定位方案，适用于增强现实和自主导航。

idea_spark.idea: 将几何先验（如深度、法线）显式融入特征融合，进一步消除渲染伪影导致的误匹配。
idea_spark.risk: 几何加权可能对噪声敏感，需验证在极端光照或动态场景下的稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从特征偏差的理论分析出发，启发其他隐式表示（如NeRF）中特征解耦的研究。

en.tldr: Proposes ULF-Loc, an unbiased landmark feature framework with geometric consistency verification for robust visual localization using 3D Gaussian Splatting, achieving lower error and higher efficiency.
en.motivation: Existing 3DGS-based localization methods suffer from feature bias due to α-blending optimization, leading to inaccurate 2D-3D matching.
en.method: Replaces biased feature optimization with geometry-weighted feature fusion, introduces keypoint-consensus landmark sampling and local geometric consistency verification.
en.result: On Cambridge Landmarks, reduces mean median translation error by 17%, with 1/10 training time and 1/6 GPU memory of STDLoc.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and robust visual localization solution for augmented reality and autonomous navigation.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly incorporate geometric priors (e.g., depth, normals) into feature fusion to further eliminate mismatches from rendering artifacts.
idea_spark.risk: Geometry weighting may be sensitive to noise; robustness under extreme lighting or dynamic scenes needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: The theoretical analysis of feature bias inspires research on feature disentanglement in other implicit representations (e.g., NeRF).

papers.title: Computational acceleration strategies for large-scale energy system optimization: a comparative study of GPU-accelerated and distributed-memory solvers
papers.summary: Energy system optimization models are increasing in scope and resolution, yielding large and challenging linear programs. For a long time, the standard way to address such problems has relied on shared-memory interior-point methods (IPM), which combine robustness and accuracy but face scalability limits as model instance size grows. Recently, two promising directions for specialized solver architectures have emerged: (i) GPU-accelerated first-order methods (FOM); and (ii) distributed-memory IPM, which can exploit block structure that arises in many energy system models. This paper presents a computational study comparing these solver classes on a diverse test set of large-scale linear programs arising from energy system analysis, including scenario-based formulations derived from stochastic programming. The results illustrate that distributed-memory IPM can leverage problem structure to deliver substantial speed-ups on specific problems with block-angular structures. GPU-accelerated FOMs demonstrate strong scalability but may yield solutions with higher relative infeasibilities, which, depending on the use case and model uncertainty, can still be acceptable. Overall, our findings indicate that recent algorithmic and hardware advances substantially broaden the computational toolbox available to the energy system optimization community. Each solver class exhibits distinct advantages: shared-memory IPMs remain a powerful tool for reliably obtaining high-accuracy solutions; distributed-memory IPMs can extend scalability to hundreds of cores for certain structured models, enabling faster time-to-solution; and GPU-based FOM can deliver fast solutions when such lower accuracy levels are appropriate. Together, they help make high-resolution, multi-scenario energy system optimization models tractable across a broader range of problem sizes and computing environments.

ai.tldr: 比较了GPU加速一阶方法和分布式内存内点法在大型能源系统优化中的计算性能。
ai.motivation: 能源系统优化模型规模日益增大，传统共享内存内点法面临可扩展性瓶颈。
ai.method: 在多种大规模线性规划测试集上，对比GPU加速一阶方法和分布式内存内点法的求解速度、精度和可扩展性。
ai.result: 分布式内存内点法对块角结构问题加速显著；GPU加速一阶方法可扩展性强但解的不满足度较高。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户根据问题结构和精度需求选择合适的求解器架构。

idea_spark.idea: 结合两种方法：先用GPU快速获得近似解，再用分布式内点法精化。
idea_spark.risk: 混合方法可能增加实现复杂度，且精度控制需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用问题结构（如块角结构）设计专用求解器可大幅提升效率。

en.tldr: Compares GPU-accelerated first-order methods and distributed-memory interior-point methods for large-scale energy system optimization.
en.motivation: Energy system optimization models are growing in size, and traditional shared-memory interior-point methods face scalability limits.
en.method: Benchmarks both solver classes on a diverse set of large-scale linear programs from energy system analysis, including scenario-based stochastic programming.
en.result: Distributed-memory IPM achieves substantial speed-ups on block-angular problems; GPU-accelerated FOM scales well but yields higher relative infeasibilities.
en.help_to_user: Helps users choose the appropriate solver architecture based on problem structure and accuracy requirements.

idea_spark.idea: Combine both methods: use GPU for a fast approximate solution, then refine with distributed IPM.
idea_spark.risk: Hybrid approach may increase implementation complexity and require careful accuracy control.
idea_spark.inspiration: Exploiting problem structure (e.g., block-angular) can dramatically improve solver efficiency.

papers.title: Attention-Based Chaotic Self-Supervision for Medical Image Classification
papers.summary: Deep learning models for medical image classification usually achieve promising results but typically rely on large, annotated datasets or standard transfer learning from ImageNet. Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) has emerged as a powerful alternative, yet common methods like masked autoencoders (MAEs) may inadvertently destroy fine-grained diagnostic features by using random masking. In this paper, we propose a novel SSL pre-training strategy, the Chaotic Denoising Autoencoder (CDAE). Instead of masking, we apply a chaotic transformation to the input image, tasking an autoencoder to reconstruct the original. We hypothesize this forces the encoder to learn robust, domain-specific features by "inverting the chaos". Furthermore, we propose an attentive fusion mechanism that combines features from our CDAE-trained encoder with a standard encoder, leveraging the strengths of both general and domain-specific representations. Our method is evaluated on two public medical datasets: ISIC 2018 (skin lesions) and APTOS 2019 (diabetic retinopathy). The proposed model achieves high performance, with an accuracy of 0.9221 and an F1-macro of 0.8530 on ISIC 2018, and an accuracy of 0.8644 and F1-macro of 0.7433 on APTOS 2019, demonstrating the efficacy of our approach.

ai.tldr: 提出混沌去噪自编码器（CDAE）用于医学图像分类的预训练，通过混沌变换替代随机掩码，结合注意力融合机制，在皮肤病变和糖尿病视网膜病变数据集上取得优异性能。
ai.motivation: 现有自监督方法（如掩码自编码器）的随机掩码可能破坏医学图像中的细粒度诊断特征，需要一种保留关键特征的预训练策略。
ai.method: 对输入图像施加混沌变换，训练自编码器重构原图；再通过注意力融合机制结合CDAE编码器与标准编码器的特征。
ai.result: 在ISIC 2018上准确率0.9221，F1-macro 0.8530；在APTOS 2019上准确率0.8644，F1-macro 0.7433。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种不依赖大规模标注数据的医学图像分类预训练方法，尤其适用于细粒度诊断任务。

idea_spark.idea: 探索更复杂的混沌变换类型（如分形噪声）或自适应混沌强度，以进一步提升特征鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 混沌变换可能引入不可逆信息损失，需验证不同变换参数对下游任务的影响。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴混沌理论中的非线性动力学，设计更贴近医学图像病理特征的变换规则。

en.tldr: Proposes Chaotic Denoising Autoencoder (CDAE) for medical image classification pre-training, replacing random masking with chaotic transformation, combined with attentive fusion, achieving strong results on skin lesion and diabetic retinopathy datasets.
en.motivation: Existing self-supervised methods like masked autoencoders may destroy fine-grained diagnostic features via random masking, necessitating a pre-training strategy that preserves key features.
en.method: Applies chaotic transformation to input images, trains an autoencoder to reconstruct the original; then uses attentive fusion to combine features from CDAE-trained encoder and a standard encoder.
en.result: Achieves accuracy 0.9221 and F1-macro 0.8530 on ISIC 2018; accuracy 0.8644 and F1-macro 0.7433 on APTOS 2019.
en.help_to_user: Provides a pre-training method for medical image classification that does not rely on large annotated datasets, especially suitable for fine-grained diagnostic tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Explore more complex chaotic transformation types (e.g., fractal noise) or adaptive chaos intensity to further improve feature robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Chaotic transformation may introduce irreversible information loss; impact of different transformation parameters on downstream tasks needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw from nonlinear dynamics in chaos theory to design transformation rules more aligned with pathological features in medical images.

papers.title: Dissociating spatial frequency reliance from adversarial robustness advantages in neurally guided deep convolutional neural networks
papers.summary: Deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) have rivaled humans on many visual tasks, yet they remain vulnerable to near-imperceptible perturbations generated by adversarial attacks. Recent work shows that aligning DCNN representations with human visual cortex activity improves adversarial robustness, but the mechanisms driving this advantage are unclear. One hypothesis suggests that neural alignment confers robustness by biasing models away from brittle high-frequency details and towards the low spatial frequencies (LSF). However, recent work shows that human object recognition critically depends on a narrow, mid-frequency "human channel". Interestingly, this band was partially preserved in prior LSF-focused studies. Here, we investigate whether a spectral bias towards the LSF or the human channel is the primary driver of the adversarial robustness observed in neurally aligned DCNNs. We first show that DCNNs aligned to higher-order regions of the human ventral visual stream systematically increase reliance on both LSF and the human channel. However, directly steering DCNNs towards these bands revealed a clear dissociation. Biasing models towards the human channel, either alone or together with LSF, does not improve robustness and even impairs it. LSF bias produced some robustness gains, but such improvements are modest despite inducing much larger shifts in spatial-frequency reliance than neurally aligned models. Spatial-frequency-biased models overall show little, if any, increase in similarity to human neural representational geometry. Together, our results suggest that altered spatial-frequency reliance is likely an emergent property of learning more human-like representations rather than the primary mechanism by which neural alignment confers adversarial robustness, and motivate the need for future research examining representational properties beyond spatial-frequency profiles.

ai.tldr: 研究发现，神经对齐的深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）的对抗鲁棒性并非主要源于对低频空间信息的偏好，而是学习更类人表征的副产品。
ai.motivation: 先前研究表明，将DCNN表征与人类视觉皮层活动对齐可提升对抗鲁棒性，但机制不明。一种假说认为对齐使模型偏向低频空间信息（LSF），但人类物体识别依赖中频“人类通道”。本文旨在区分LSF偏好与人类通道偏好对鲁棒性的贡献。
ai.method: 首先分析对齐高级腹侧视觉流区域的DCNN对LSF和人类通道的依赖变化；然后直接引导模型偏向这些频带，观察鲁棒性变化。
ai.result: 对齐模型同时增加了对LSF和人类通道的依赖；但直接偏向人类通道不提升鲁棒性甚至损害之；LSF偏好带来适度鲁棒性提升，但远小于对齐模型的效果；频带偏置模型与人类神经表征相似度低。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解神经对齐提升对抗鲁棒性的机制，指出空间频率偏置并非主因，为设计更鲁棒的视觉模型提供新视角。

idea_spark.idea: 探索除空间频率外的其他表征属性（如纹理、形状）在神经对齐鲁棒性中的作用。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖特定频带可能忽略其他重要表征维度，导致鲁棒性提升有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类视觉系统多尺度处理启发，可设计多频带联合优化的训练策略。

idea_spark.idea: 探索除空间频率外的其他表征属性（如纹理、形状）在神经对齐鲁棒性中的作用。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖特定频带可能忽略其他重要表征维度，导致鲁棒性提升有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类视觉系统多尺度处理启发，可设计多频带联合优化的训练策略。

en.tldr: The study finds that adversarial robustness in neurally aligned DCNNs is not primarily due to low spatial frequency bias, but emerges as a byproduct of learning more human-like representations.
en.motivation: Prior work shows aligning DCNN representations with human visual cortex improves adversarial robustness, but the mechanism is unclear. One hypothesis suggests bias toward low spatial frequencies (LSF), yet human object recognition relies on a mid-frequency 'human channel'. This work dissociates the contributions of LSF vs. human channel bias.
en.method: Analyzed spatial frequency reliance in DCNNs aligned to higher-order ventral stream regions; then directly biased models toward LSF or human channel and measured robustness.
en.result: Aligned models increase reliance on both LSF and human channel; direct human channel bias does not improve robustness and even impairs it; LSF bias yields modest gains but far less than aligned models; frequency-biased models show low neural similarity.
en.help_to_user: Clarifies that spatial frequency bias is not the primary driver of robustness from neural alignment, guiding future research toward other representational properties.

idea_spark.idea: Investigate other representational properties (e.g., texture, shape) in neural alignment robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Overemphasizing specific frequency bands may neglect other important dimensions, limiting robustness gains.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human multi-scale processing, design training strategies that jointly optimize multiple frequency bands.

papers.title: SAMIC: A Lightweight Semantic-Aware Mamba for Efficient Perceptual Image Compression
papers.summary: Perceptual image compression focuses on preserving high visual quality under low-bitrate constraints. Most existing approaches to perceptual compression leverage the strong generative capabilities of generative adversarial networks or diffusion models, at the cost of substantial model complexity. To this end, we present an efficient perceptual image compression method that exploits the long-range modeling capability and linear computational complexity of state space models, with a particular focus on Mamba. Unlike existing methods that rely on an inherently fixed scanning order and consequently impair semantic continuity and spatial correlation, we develop a semantic-aware Mamba block (SAMB) to enable scanning guided by dynamically clustered semantic features, thereby alleviating the strict causality constraints and long-range information decay inherent to Mamba. Inspired by singular value decomposition, we design an SVD-inspired redundancy reduction module (SVD-RRM) that performs a low-rank approximation on the latent features by introducing a learnable soft threshold, leading to channel-wise redundancy information reduction. The proposed SAMB is integrated into both the encoder and decoder of the compression framework, whereas the SVD-RRM is incorporated only in the encoder. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method performs favorably against state-of-the-art approaches in terms of rate-distortion-perception tradeoff and model complexity. The source code and pretrained models will be available at https://github.com/Jasmine-aiq/SAMIC.

ai.tldr: 提出一种轻量级语义感知Mamba图像压缩方法SAMIC，在低码率下保持高视觉质量，同时降低模型复杂度。
ai.motivation: 现有感知压缩方法依赖GAN或扩散模型，计算复杂度过高；而Mamba虽具有线性复杂度，但其固定扫描顺序破坏了语义连续性和空间相关性。
ai.method: 设计语义感知Mamba块（SAMB），利用动态聚类语义特征引导扫描；提出SVD启发冗余缩减模块（SVD-RRM），通过可学习软阈值对潜在特征进行低秩近似以减少通道冗余。
ai.result: 在率失真感知权衡和模型复杂度上优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、轻量的图像压缩方案，适合资源受限场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将SAMB与SVD-RRM结合，在编码器中同时实现语义引导和冗余消除。
idea_spark.risk: 语义聚类可能引入额外计算开销；低秩近似可能丢失高频细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用状态空间模型的线性复杂度，通过动态扫描顺序和低秩约束实现高效压缩。

zh.tldr: 提出一种轻量级语义感知Mamba图像压缩方法SAMIC，在低码率下保持高视觉质量，同时降低模型复杂度。
zh.motivation: 现有感知压缩方法依赖GAN或扩散模型，计算复杂度过高；而Mamba虽具有线性复杂度，但其固定扫描顺序破坏了语义连续性和空间相关性。

zh.result: 在率失真感知权衡和模型复杂度上优于现有方法。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、轻量的图像压缩方案，适合资源受限场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将SAMB与SVD-RRM结合，在编码器中同时实现语义引导和冗余消除。
idea_spark.risk: 语义聚类可能引入额外计算开销；低秩近似可能丢失高频细节。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用状态空间模型的线性复杂度，通过动态扫描顺序和低秩约束实现高效压缩。

en.tldr: Proposes SAMIC, a lightweight semantic-aware Mamba method for efficient perceptual image compression, achieving high visual quality at low bitrates with reduced model complexity.
en.motivation: Existing perceptual compression methods rely on GANs or diffusion models with high computational cost; Mamba offers linear complexity but its fixed scanning order impairs semantic continuity and spatial correlation.
en.method: Designs a semantic-aware Mamba block (SAMB) that uses dynamically clustered semantic features to guide scanning, and an SVD-inspired redundancy reduction module (SVD-RRM) that performs low-rank approximation on latent features via a learnable soft threshold.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art methods in rate-distortion-perception tradeoff and model complexity.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and lightweight image compression solution suitable for resource-constrained scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Combines SAMB and SVD-RRM in the encoder to achieve both semantic guidance and redundancy reduction.
idea_spark.risk: Semantic clustering may introduce extra computational overhead; low-rank approximation may lose high-frequency details.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverages the linear complexity of state space models with dynamic scanning order and low-rank constraints for efficient compression.

papers.title: Anny-Fit: All-Age Human Mesh Recovery
papers.summary: Recovering 3D human pose and shape from a single image remains a cornerstone of human-centric vision, yet most methods assume adult subjects and optimize each person independently. These assumptions fail in real-world, all-age scenes, where body proportions and depth must be resolved jointly. We introduce Anny-Fit, a multi-person, camera-space optimization framework for all-age 3D human mesh recovery (HMR). Unlike existing per-person fitting methods, Anny-Fit jointly optimizes all individuals directly in the camera coordinate system, enforcing global spatial consistency. At the core of our approach is the use of multiple forms of expert knowledge -- including metric depth maps, instance segmentation, 2D keypoints, and, VLM-derived semantic attributes such as age and gender -- each obtained from dedicated off-the-shelf networks. These complementary signals jointly guide the optimization, constraining the depth-scale ambiguity characteristic of all-age scenes. Across diverse datasets, Anny-Fit consistently improves 2D reprojection accuracy (+13 to 16), relative depth ordering (+6 to 7), 3D estimation error (-9 to -29) and shape estimation (+25 to +82), producing more coherent scenes. Finally, we show that VLM-based semantic knowledge can be distilled into an HMR model via the pseudo-ground-truth annotations produced by Anny-Fit on training data, enabling it to learn semantically meaningful shape parameters while improving HMR performance. Our approach bridges adult-only and all-age modeling by enabling zero-shot adaptation of adult-trained HMR pipelines to the full age spectrum without retraining. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/naver/anny-fit.

ai.tldr: 提出Anny-Fit，一种面向全年龄段的多人体网格恢复框架，通过联合优化和多种专家知识解决深度尺度歧义。
ai.motivation: 现有方法假设成人主体并独立优化每个人，无法处理真实场景中全年龄段的体型比例和深度歧义。
ai.method: 在相机坐标系中联合优化所有人，融合度量深度图、实例分割、2D关键点和VLM语义属性（年龄、性别）等专家知识。
ai.result: 在多个数据集上，2D重投影精度提升13-16，相对深度排序提升6-7，3D估计误差降低9-29，形状估计提升25-82。
ai.help_to_user: 提供全年龄段人体网格恢复的零样本适应方案，无需重新训练即可将成人模型扩展到所有年龄段。

idea_spark.idea: 利用VLM生成伪真值标注，将语义知识蒸馏到HMR模型中，提升形状参数语义性。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖多个离线网络，可能引入累积误差；VLM语义属性在极端年龄或遮挡下可能不准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将语义先验（如年龄）融入几何优化，启发其他领域利用大模型知识增强低层视觉任务。

zh.help_to_user: 提供全年龄段人体网格恢复的零样本适应方案，无需重新训练即可将成人模型扩展到所有年龄段。

idea_spark.idea: 利用VLM生成伪真值标注，将语义知识蒸馏到HMR模型中，提升形状参数语义性。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖多个离线网络，可能引入累积误差；VLM语义属性在极端年龄或遮挡下可能不准确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将语义先验（如年龄）融入几何优化，启发其他领域利用大模型知识增强低层视觉任务。

en.tldr: Anny-Fit is a multi-person camera-space optimization framework for all-age 3D human mesh recovery, jointly optimizing all individuals with multiple expert knowledge sources.
en.motivation: Existing methods assume adult subjects and optimize each person independently, failing in real-world all-age scenes with varying body proportions and depth ambiguity.
en.method: Joint optimization in camera coordinates using metric depth maps, instance segmentation, 2D keypoints, and VLM-derived semantic attributes (age, gender) from off-the-shelf networks.
en.result: Across datasets, 2D reprojection accuracy improves by 13-16, relative depth ordering by 6-7, 3D estimation error reduces by 9-29, and shape estimation improves by 25-82.
en.help_to_user: Enables zero-shot adaptation of adult-trained HMR pipelines to all ages without retraining, producing coherent scenes.

idea_spark.idea: Distill VLM semantic knowledge into HMR models via pseudo-ground-truth annotations to learn semantically meaningful shape parameters.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on multiple off-the-shelf networks may accumulate errors; VLM attributes may be inaccurate for extreme ages or occlusions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporating semantic priors (e.g., age) into geometric optimization inspires leveraging large model knowledge to enhance low-level vision tasks.

papers.title: Driver-WM: A Driver-Centric Traffic-Conditioned Latent World Model for In-Cabin Dynamics Rollout
papers.summary: Safe L2/L3 driving automation requires anticipating human-in-the-loop reactions during shared-control transitions. While most driving world models forecast the external environment, in-cabin intelligence remains strictly recognition-oriented and lacks multi-step rollout capabilities for driver dynamics. We introduce Driver-WM, a driver-centric latent world model that rolls out in-cabin dynamics causally conditioned on out-cabin traffic context. This formulation unifies physical kinematics forecasting with auxiliary behavioral and emotional semantic recognition. Operating in a compact latent space constructed from frozen vision-language features, Driver-WM adopts a dual-stream architecture to separately encode external traffic and internal driver states. These streams are directionally coupled via a gated causal injection mechanism, which uses a learned vector gate to modulate external contextual perturbations while strictly enforcing temporal causality. Evaluations on a multi-task assistive driving benchmark demonstrate that Driver-WM yields robust long-horizon geometric forecasting for reactive high-motion maneuvers and improves semantic alignment for both driver and traffic states. Finally, the explicit external-to-internal conditioning allows for controlled test-time interventions to systematically analyze mechanism responses.

ai.tldr: 提出Driver-WM，一种以驾驶员为中心的潜在世界模型，能够在交通条件下滚动预测车内动态，统一物理运动预测与行为情感识别。
ai.motivation: L2/L3级自动驾驶需要预测人机交互反应，但现有模型只关注外部环境，车内智能缺乏多步预测能力。
ai.method: 在冻结的视觉语言潜在空间中，采用双流架构分别编码外部交通和内部驾驶员状态，通过门控因果注入机制实现方向性耦合。
ai.result: 在多任务辅助驾驶基准上，实现了鲁棒的长时域几何预测，并改善了驾驶员与交通状态的语义对齐。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解驾驶员在共享控制中的动态反应，提升自动驾驶系统的安全性和人机协同能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将驾驶员状态预测与交通场景因果耦合，实现可控的测试时干预，分析系统响应机制。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖冻结的视觉语言特征，可能限制对细粒度驾驶员行为的建模；因果注入的泛化性需更多验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受世界模型在外部环境预测中的成功启发，将其扩展至车内人机交互的动态预测。

idea_spark.idea: 将驾驶员状态预测与交通场景因果耦合，实现可控的测试时干预，分析系统响应机制。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖冻结的视觉语言特征，可能限制对细粒度驾驶员行为的建模；因果注入的泛化性需更多验证。

en.tldr: Proposes Driver-WM, a driver-centric latent world model that rolls out in-cabin dynamics conditioned on traffic context, unifying physical kinematics forecasting with behavioral and emotional recognition.
en.motivation: L2/L3 driving automation requires anticipating human-in-the-loop reactions during shared-control transitions, but existing models focus on external environment while in-cabin intelligence lacks multi-step rollout.
en.method: Operates in a compact latent space from frozen vision-language features, using a dual-stream architecture to encode external traffic and internal driver states, coupled via a gated causal injection mechanism.
en.result: Achieves robust long-horizon geometric forecasting for reactive maneuvers and improves semantic alignment for driver and traffic states on a multi-task assistive driving benchmark.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand driver dynamics in shared control, enhancing safety and human-machine cooperation in autonomous driving.

idea_spark.idea: Couple driver state prediction with traffic scene causality, enabling controlled test-time interventions to analyze system response mechanisms.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on frozen vision-language features may limit fine-grained driver behavior modeling; generalization of causal injection needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of world models in external environment forecasting, extending them to in-cabin human-machine interaction dynamics.

papers.title: Decision Problems in Multilevel Linear Programming
papers.summary: We study the computational complexity of decision problems in $k$-level linear programming (LP). Seminal work by Jeroslow establishes that determining whether the optimal objective value of a $k$-level LP is at least as good as a given threshold is $Σ^{\mathrm{p}}_{k-1}$-hard. In this paper, we demonstrate the matching upper bound and thereby prove that this problem is $Σ^{\mathrm{p}}_{k-1}$-complete. To this end, we show that the feasible region of a $k$-level LP can be expressed as a union of sets defined by weak and strict linear inequalities. Moreover, we show that the decision of the unboundedness is $Σ^{\mathrm{p}}_{k-1}$-complete. Finally, we discuss the extension of our results to the mixed-binary cases. In short, this work closes lasting open questions in multilevel programming.

ai.tldr: 证明了k级线性规划决策问题的Σ^{p}_{k-1}-完全性，包括最优值判定和无界性判定，并推广到混合二元情形。
ai.motivation: Jeroslow的开创性工作仅给出了k级线性规划决策问题的下界（Σ^{p}_{k-1}-难），但上界长期未知。本文旨在证明匹配的上界，从而彻底解决该问题的精确复杂度。
ai.method: 通过将k级线性规划的可行域表示为弱和严格线性不等式定义的集合之并，并利用多项式时间归约，证明最优值判定和无界性判定均为Σ^{p}_{k-1}-完全。
ai.result: 证明了k级线性规划的最优值判定问题是Σ^{p}_{k-1}-完全的，无界性判定也是Σ^{p}_{k-1}-完全的，并将结果推广到混合二元情形。
ai.help_to_user: 为多级线性规划问题的算法设计和近似求解提供了精确的复杂度基准，帮助研究者避免对NP完全以上问题寻求多项式时间精确算法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Σ^{p}_{k-1}完全性的证明框架，可以设计针对特定结构多级线性规划问题的启发式算法或近似算法，例如通过限制可行域形状来降低实际计算难度。
idea_spark.risk: 多级线性规划在实际应用中规模可能很大，即使复杂度已知，精确求解仍可能指数级困难，需谨慎评估近似或启发式方法的有效性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受可行域分解思想启发，可尝试将多级决策问题转化为一系列单级子问题，利用层次结构设计分布式或迭代求解方法。

zh.tldr: 证明了k级线性规划决策问题的Σ^{p}_{k-1}-完全性，包括最优值判定和无界性判定，并推广到混合二元情形。
zh.motivation: Jeroslow的开创性工作仅给出了k级线性规划决策问题的下界（Σ^{p}_{k-1}-难），但上界长期未知。本文旨在证明匹配的上界，从而彻底解决该问题的精确复杂度。
zh.method: 通过将k级线性规划的可行域表示为弱和严格线性不等式定义的集合之并，并利用多项式时间归约，证明最优值判定和无界性判定均为Σ^{p}_{k-1}-完全。
zh.result: 证明了k级线性规划的最优值判定问题是Σ^{p}_{k-1}-完全的，无界性判定也是Σ^{p}_{k-1}-完全的，并将结果推广到混合二元情形。
zh.help_to_user: 为多级线性规划问题的算法设计和近似求解提供了精确的复杂度基准，帮助研究者避免对NP完全以上问题寻求多项式时间精确算法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Σ^{p}_{k-1}完全性的证明框架，可以设计针对特定结构多级线性规划问题的启发式算法或近似算法，例如通过限制可行域形状来降低实际计算难度。
idea_spark.risk: 多级线性规划在实际应用中规模可能很大，即使复杂度已知，精确求解仍可能指数级困难，需谨慎评估近似或启发式方法的有效性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受可行域分解思想启发，可尝试将多级决策问题转化为一系列单级子问题，利用层次结构设计分布式或迭代求解方法。

en.tldr: Proves Σ^{p}_{k-1}-completeness of decision problems in k-level linear programming, including optimal value and unboundedness, with extension to mixed-binary cases.
en.motivation: Jeroslow's seminal work only established a lower bound (Σ^{p}_{k-1}-hard) for decision problems in k-level LP; the matching upper bound remained open. This paper aims to close this gap and settle the exact complexity.
en.method: By expressing the feasible region of a k-level LP as a union of sets defined by weak and strict linear inequalities, and using polynomial-time reductions, we prove that both optimal value and unboundedness decisions are Σ^{p}_{k-1}-complete.
en.result: We show that deciding whether the optimal objective value of a k-level LP is at least a given threshold is Σ^{p}_{k-1}-complete, and that unboundedness is also Σ^{p}_{k-1}-complete. The results extend to mixed-binary cases.
en.help_to_user: Provides a precise complexity benchmark for multilevel linear programming, guiding researchers to avoid seeking polynomial-time exact algorithms for problems above NP-complete.

idea_spark.idea: Leveraging the Σ^{p}_{k-1}-completeness proof framework, one can design heuristic or approximation algorithms for specific structured multilevel LP instances, e.g., by restricting feasible region shapes to reduce practical difficulty.
idea_spark.risk: Multilevel LP problems can be large-scale in practice; even with known complexity, exact solving may remain exponentially hard. The effectiveness of approximation or heuristic methods must be carefully evaluated.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the feasible region decomposition, one could attempt to transform multilevel decision problems into a series of single-level subproblems, leveraging the hierarchical structure to design distributed or iterative solution methods.

papers.title: LoViF 2026 The First Challenge on Holistic Quality Assessment for 4D World Model (PhyScore)
papers.summary: This paper reports on the LoViF 2026 PhyScore challenge, a competition on holistic quality assessment of world-model-generated videos across both 2D and 4D generation settings. The challenge is motivated by a central gap in current evaluation practice: perceptual quality alone is insufficient to judge whether generated dynamics are physically plausible, temporally coherent, and consistent with input conditions. Participants are required to build a metric that jointly predicts four dimensions, i.e., Video Quality, Physical Realism, Condition-Video Alignment, and Temporal Consistency. Depart from that, participants also need to localize physical anomaly timestamps for fine-grained diagnosis.   The benchmark dataset contains 1,554 videos generated by seven representative world generative models, organized into three tracks (text-2D, image-to-4D, and video-to-4D) and spanning 26 categories. These categories explicitly cover physics-relevant scenarios, including dynamics, optics, and thermodynamics, together with diverse real-world and creative content. To ensure label reliability, scores and anomaly timestamps are produced through trained human annotation with an additional automated quality-control pass.   Evaluation is based on both score prediction and anomaly localization, with a composite protocol that combines TimeStamp_IOU and SRCC/PLCC. This report summarizes the challenge design and provides method-level insights from submitted solutions.

ai.tldr: 本文报告了LoViF 2026 PhyScore挑战赛，旨在评估世界模型生成视频的整体质量，包括视频质量、物理真实性、条件-视频对齐和时间一致性，并定位物理异常时间戳。
ai.motivation: 当前评估实践仅关注感知质量，不足以判断生成动态的物理合理性、时间一致性和条件一致性，存在关键空白。
ai.method: 参赛者需构建联合预测四个维度（视频质量、物理真实性、条件-视频对齐、时间一致性）的指标，并定位物理异常时间戳。数据集包含1554个视频，来自7个世界生成模型，涵盖3个赛道和26个类别。
ai.result: 评估采用分数预测和异常定位的复合协议，结合TimeStamp_IOU和SRCC/PLCC指标。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何全面评估世界模型生成视频的质量，特别是物理合理性和时间一致性。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一个联合预测多个质量维度并定位异常时间戳的指标，可启发新的视频生成评估方法。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖人工标注和自动质量控制，可能引入主观偏差；异常定位的精确性受限于标注质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理合理性作为评估维度，可推动生成模型在物理模拟领域的应用，如自动驾驶、机器人仿真。

en.tldr: This paper reports the LoViF 2026 PhyScore challenge, which aims to holistically assess the quality of world-model-generated videos, including video quality, physical realism, condition-video alignment, and temporal consistency, along with localizing physical anomaly timestamps.
en.motivation: Current evaluation practice focuses only on perceptual quality, which is insufficient to judge whether generated dynamics are physically plausible, temporally coherent, and consistent with input conditions.
en.method: Participants must build a metric that jointly predicts four dimensions (video quality, physical realism, condition-video alignment, temporal consistency) and localizes physical anomaly timestamps. The dataset includes 1,554 videos from 7 world generative models across 3 tracks and 26 categories.
en.result: Evaluation uses a composite protocol combining TimeStamp_IOU and SRCC/PLCC for score prediction and anomaly localization.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to comprehensively evaluate the quality of world-model-generated videos, especially physical plausibility and temporal consistency.

idea_spark.idea: Designing a metric that jointly predicts multiple quality dimensions and localizes anomaly timestamps can inspire new video generation evaluation methods.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on human annotation and automated quality control may introduce subjective bias; anomaly localization accuracy is limited by annotation quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporating physical plausibility as an evaluation dimension can drive the application of generative models in physical simulation domains, such as autonomous driving and robot simulation.

papers.title: Reference-based Category Discovery: Unsupervised Object Detection with Category Awareness
papers.summary: Traditional one-shot detection methods have addressed the closed-set problem in object detection, but the high cost of data annotation remains a critical challenge. General unsupervised methods generate pseudo boxes without category labels, thus failing to achieve category-aware classification. To overcome these limitations, we propose Reference-based Category Discovery (RefCD), an unsupervised detector that enables category-aware\footnotemark[1] detection without any manually annotated labels. It leverages feature similarity between predicted objects and unlabeled reference images. Unlike previous unsupervised methods that lack category guidance and one-shot methods which require labeled data, RefCD introduces a carefully designed feature similarity loss to explicitly guide the learning of potential category-specific features. Additionally, RefCD supports category-agnostic detection without reference images, serving as a unified framework. Comprehensive quantitative and qualitative analysis of category-aware and category-agnostic detection results demonstrates its effectiveness, and RefCD can learn category information in an unsupervised paradigm even without category labels.

ai.tldr: 提出基于参考的类别发现方法，无需标注即可实现类别感知的目标检测。
ai.motivation: 传统无监督检测无法区分类别，单样本方法依赖标注，数据标注成本高。
ai.method: 利用预测目标与未标注参考图像的特征相似性损失，引导学习潜在类别特征。
ai.result: 在类别感知和类别无关检测上均有效，无需类别标签即可学习类别信息。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需人工标注即可进行类别感知目标检测的思路，降低数据成本。

idea_spark.idea: 将参考图像扩展为动态更新的类别原型库，增强类别特征稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 参考图像质量或类别分布不均可能影响检测性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用无标签参考图像作为类别锚点，启发无监督细粒度分类或开放世界检测。

en.tldr: Proposes Reference-based Category Discovery for unsupervised category-aware object detection without manual labels.
en.motivation: Unsupervised detectors lack category guidance; one-shot methods require labeled data; annotation cost is high.
en.method: Uses feature similarity loss between predicted objects and unlabeled reference images to learn category-specific features.
en.result: Effective for both category-aware and category-agnostic detection; learns category information without labels.
en.help_to_user: Offers a label-free approach to category-aware detection, reducing annotation effort.

idea_spark.idea: Extend reference images to a dynamically updated category prototype library for better stability.
idea_spark.risk: Quality or distribution of reference images may affect detection performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using unlabeled reference images as category anchors inspires unsupervised fine-grained classification or open-world detection.

papers.title: Text Corpora as Concept Fields: Black-Box Hallucination and Novelty Measurement
papers.summary: We introduce the **Concept Field** of a text corpus: a local drift field with pointwise uncertainty, estimated in sentence-embedding space from the deltas between consecutive sentences. Given a candidate sentence transition, we score its agreement with the field by $ζ$, the mean absolute z-distance between the observed delta and the field's local Gaussian estimate. The score is black-box (no model internals), corpus-attributable (every score traces to nearby corpus sentences), and admits a direct probabilistic reading. We support the computation with the introduction of a **Vector Sequence Database (VSDB)** that stores embeddings together with sequence-position and next-delta metadata. We evaluate this approach on two large-scale settings: hallucination-style groundedness detection over the U.S. Code of Federal Regulations, and novelty detection over Project Gutenberg. Using controlled LLM-generated rewrites, Concept Fields achieve strong selective classification performance under a grounded / ungrounded / unsure triage policy, which unlike retrieval-centric baselines have similar coverage-risk behavior across both domains, supporting a probability-based interpretation that transfers across domains. We also sketch how divergence and curl of the Concept Field, computed on dense clusters, surface qualitatively meaningful semantic patterns (logic sources, sinks, and implicit topics), which we offer as hypothesis-generating rather than as a quantitative result. Concept Fields provide a fast, lightweight, and interpretable signal for groundedness and novelty, complementary to LLM-as-judge and white-box detectors.

ai.tldr: 提出文本语料库的“概念场”，通过句子嵌入空间中连续句子的差异估计局部漂移场，用于黑盒幻觉和新颖性检测。
ai.motivation: 现有幻觉和新颖性检测方法依赖模型内部信息或检索，缺乏轻量、可解释且跨域通用的黑盒信号。
ai.method: 从语料库句子嵌入中计算连续句子间的增量，拟合局部高斯分布，用ζ分数（平均绝对z距离）衡量候选句子转换与场的匹配度。引入向量序列数据库（VSDB）存储嵌入及位置、增量元数据。
ai.result: 在美国联邦法规和古腾堡计划数据集上，概念场在幻觉检测和新颖性检测中实现强选择性分类，优于检索基线，且概率解释跨域迁移。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需模型内部信息、快速轻量的文本可信度与新颖性评估工具，可辅助LLM输出验证。

idea_spark.idea: 利用概念场的散度和旋度分析密集簇，揭示语义模式（逻辑源、汇和隐含主题），可作为假设生成工具。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖句子嵌入质量，对嵌入空间敏感；散度/旋度分析为定性结果，需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理场概念引入文本分析，启发其他基于嵌入空间的语义流建模方法。

en.tldr: Introduces Concept Fields for text corpora, a local drift field estimated from sentence embedding deltas, enabling black-box hallucination and novelty detection.
en.motivation: Existing hallucination and novelty detection methods rely on model internals or retrieval, lacking a lightweight, interpretable, and cross-domain black-box signal.
en.method: Estimates a local Gaussian drift field from consecutive sentence embedding deltas in a corpus; scores candidate transitions via ζ (mean absolute z-distance). Introduces Vector Sequence Database (VSDB) for efficient storage.
en.result: On U.S. Code of Federal Regulations and Project Gutenberg, Concept Fields achieve strong selective classification for hallucination and novelty detection, outperforming retrieval baselines with cross-domain transferable probability interpretation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a fast, lightweight, and interpretable black-box signal for assessing text groundedness and novelty, complementary to LLM-as-judge methods.

idea_spark.idea: Using divergence and curl of the Concept Field on dense clusters to surface semantic patterns (logic sources, sinks, implicit topics) as hypothesis generators.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on sentence embedding quality; divergence/curl analysis is qualitative and requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrowing physical field concepts for text analysis inspires other embedding-space semantic flow modeling approaches.

papers.title: Adaptive Contention-based Random Access for Uplink Reporting in 3GPP Ambient IoT Networks
papers.summary: Ambient Internet of Things (A-IoT) targets energy harvesting (EH), battery-less devices as a simple connectivity solution for extensive ultra-low-power deployments. These devices typically face intermittent energy availability, making uplink reports increasingly susceptible to access collisions and energy outages. In this paper, we build upon the cellular standardization of A-IoT and examine the paging-triggered contention-based random access (CBRA) framework for uplink reporting. We analyze the effects of energy availability and collisions on these systems and introduce an EH-aware access control mechanism. In this mechanism, the reader broadcasts an access probability in the paging message, which helps regulate the number of devices attempting random access. Results show that, unlike the baselines, the proposed method scales well under dense deployments by keeping collisions nearly constant, improving access efficiency, and substantially reducing the number of paging rounds required for successful reporting. These results highlight the importance of lightweight reader-side access control for reliable and resource-efficient reporting in A-IoT environments.

ai.tldr: 针对3GPP环境物联网中能量采集设备的间歇性能量问题，提出一种基于分页触发的自适应竞争随机接入机制，通过广播接入概率控制设备数量，减少碰撞并提升接入效率。
ai.motivation: 环境物联网设备能量不稳定，上行报告易受接入碰撞和能量中断影响，现有方法在密集部署下扩展性差。
ai.method: 在分页消息中广播接入概率，由读取器动态调节尝试随机接入的设备数量，实现能量感知的接入控制。
ai.result: 与基线相比，所提方法在密集部署下碰撞率几乎恒定，接入效率显著提升，所需分页轮次大幅减少。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过轻量级读取器侧接入控制实现可靠、资源高效的环境物联网上行报告。

idea_spark.idea: 结合能量预测动态调整接入概率，或引入多优先级接入概率以区分不同紧急程度的报告。
idea_spark.risk: 广播接入概率可能增加信令开销，且对信道估计误差敏感，需评估实际部署中的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从能量采集设备的间歇性特征出发，将能量状态作为接入控制的反馈信号，启发其他间歇性资源受限系统的调度设计。

en.tldr: For 3GPP Ambient IoT with energy-harvesting devices, an adaptive contention-based random access mechanism is proposed, using a broadcast access probability in paging messages to regulate device attempts, reducing collisions and improving access efficiency.
en.motivation: Ambient IoT devices suffer from intermittent energy, making uplink reports prone to access collisions and energy outages; existing methods scale poorly under dense deployments.
en.method: The reader broadcasts an access probability in the paging message to dynamically control the number of devices attempting random access, enabling energy-aware access control.
en.result: Compared to baselines, the proposed method keeps collisions nearly constant under dense deployments, significantly improves access efficiency, and reduces the number of paging rounds required.
en.help_to_user: Understand how lightweight reader-side access control enables reliable and resource-efficient uplink reporting in Ambient IoT environments.

idea_spark.idea: Combine energy prediction to dynamically adjust access probability, or introduce multi-priority access probabilities for reports with different urgency.
idea_spark.risk: Broadcasting access probability may increase signaling overhead and is sensitive to channel estimation errors; robustness in practical deployments needs evaluation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging the intermittent energy characteristic of energy-harvesting devices as a feedback signal for access control inspires scheduling designs for other intermittently resource-constrained systems.

papers.title: Detecting Deepfakes via Hamiltonian Dynamics
papers.summary: Driven by the rapid development of generative AI models, deepfake detectors are compelled to undergo periodic recalibration to capture newly developed synthetic artifacts. To break this cycle, we propose a new perspective on deepfake detection: moving from static pattern recognition to dynamical stability analysis. Specifically, our approach is motivated by physics-inspired priors: we hypothesize that natural images, as products of dissipative physical processes, tend to settle near stable, low-energy equilibria. In contrast, generative models optimize for statistical similarity to real images but do not explicitly enforce structural constraints such as geometric smoothness, leaving deepfakes more likely to occupy unstable, high-energy states. To operationalize this, we introduce Hamiltonian Action Anomaly Detection (HAAD), comprising three contributions: \textbf{i)} We model the image latent manifold as a potential energy surface. Under this hypothesis, real images are expected to produce basin-like low-energy responses, whereas fake images are more likely to induce high-potential, high-gradient responses. \textbf{ii)} We employ Hamiltonian-inspired dynamics as a stability probe. By releasing latent states from rest, samples near stable regions remain bounded, while high-gradient samples produce larger trajectory responses. \textbf{iii)} We quantify these dynamic behaviors through two trajectory statistics, \ie, Hamiltonian action and energy dissipation. Extensive experiments show that HAAD outperforms evaluated state-of-the-art baselines on challenging cross-dataset transfer benchmarks, supporting a physics-inspired stability prior for digital forensics.

ai.tldr: 提出基于哈密顿动力学的深度伪造检测方法，利用物理稳定性先验区分真实与伪造图像。
ai.motivation: 现有检测器需频繁更新以应对新合成伪影，本文从静态模式识别转向动态稳定性分析，引入物理先验打破这一循环。
ai.method: 将图像潜在流形建模为势能面，通过哈密顿动力学探测样本稳定性，计算轨迹统计量（哈密顿作用与能量耗散）进行异常检测。
ai.result: 在跨数据集迁移基准上优于现有方法，验证了物理启发的稳定性先验的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种不依赖特定伪影的深度伪造检测新思路，可提升跨域泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将图像潜在空间视为物理系统，通过动力学响应而非静态特征进行异常检测。
idea_spark.risk: 对势能面建模的准确性敏感，可能受限于潜在空间的质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受哈密顿力学中能量守恒与轨迹稳定性启发，将物理概念引入数字取证。

idea_spark.risk: 对势能面建模的准确性敏感，可能受限于潜在空间的质量。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受哈密顿力学中能量守恒与轨迹稳定性启发，将物理概念引入数字取证。

en.tldr: Proposes Hamiltonian dynamics-based deepfake detection, leveraging physical stability priors to distinguish real from fake images.
en.motivation: Existing detectors require frequent updates for new synthetic artifacts; this work shifts from static pattern recognition to dynamical stability analysis, introducing physics-inspired priors to break the cycle.
en.method: Models the image latent manifold as a potential energy surface, probes stability via Hamiltonian dynamics, and computes trajectory statistics (Hamiltonian action and energy dissipation) for anomaly detection.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on cross-dataset transfer benchmarks, validating the effectiveness of physics-inspired stability priors.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new deepfake detection paradigm independent of specific artifacts, enhancing cross-domain generalization.

idea_spark.idea: Treats the image latent space as a physical system, detecting anomalies via dynamical response rather than static features.
idea_spark.risk: Sensitive to the accuracy of potential energy surface modeling, potentially limited by latent space quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by energy conservation and trajectory stability in Hamiltonian mechanics, applying physical concepts to digital forensics.

papers.title: D-OPSD: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Continuously Tuning Step-Distilled Diffusion Models
papers.summary: The landscape of high-performance image generation models is currently shifting from the inefficient multi-step ones to the efficient few-step counterparts (e.g, Z-Image-Turbo and FLUX.2-klein). However, these models present significant challenges for directly continuous supervised fine-tuning. For example, applying the commonly used fine-tuning technique would compromises their inherent few-step inference capability. To address this, we propose D-OPSD, a novel training paradigm for step-distilled diffusion models that enables on-policy learning during supervised fine-tuning. We first find that the modern diffusion model where the LLM/VLM serves as the encoder can inherit its encoder's in-context capabilities. This enables us to make the training as an on-policy self-distillation process. Specifically, during training, we make the model acts as both the teacher and the student with different contexts, where the student is conditioned only on the text feature, while the teacher is conditioned on the multimodal feature of both the text prompt and the target image. Training minimizes the two predicted distributions over the student's own roll-outs. By optimized on the model's own trajectory and under it's own supervision, D-OPSD enables the model to learn new concept, style, etc. without sacrificing the original few-step capacity.

ai.tldr: 提出D-OPSD，一种用于步蒸馏扩散模型的在线策略自蒸馏训练范式，在不牺牲少步推理能力的前提下实现连续监督微调。
ai.motivation: 现有少步扩散模型（如Z-Image-Turbo、FLUX.2-klein）难以直接进行连续监督微调，常用微调技术会破坏其固有的少步推理能力。
ai.method: 利用现代扩散模型编码器（LLM/VLM）的上下文能力，使模型在训练中同时扮演教师和学生：学生仅依赖文本特征，教师依赖文本与目标图像的多模态特征，通过最小化学生自身轨迹上的两个预测分布进行自蒸馏。
ai.result: 模型能在自身轨迹和自监督下学习新概念、风格等，同时保持原有的少步生成能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效微调少步扩散模型，使其适应新任务或风格而不损失推理速度。

idea_spark.idea: 将LLM/VLM编码器的上下文能力与自蒸馏结合，实现教师-学生角色动态切换。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖编码器的上下文能力，若编码器能力不足可能影响蒸馏效果；训练计算成本较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模型自身轨迹进行自监督，避免外部教师网络，降低对额外标注的依赖。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖编码器的上下文能力，若编码器能力不足可能影响蒸馏效果；训练计算成本较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用模型自身轨迹进行自监督，避免外部教师网络，降低对额外标注的依赖。

en.tldr: Proposes D-OPSD, an on-policy self-distillation training paradigm for step-distilled diffusion models, enabling continuous supervised fine-tuning without sacrificing few-step inference capability.
en.motivation: Existing few-step diffusion models (e.g., Z-Image-Turbo, FLUX.2-klein) are difficult to directly fine-tune via continuous supervised learning, as common fine-tuning techniques compromise their inherent few-step inference ability.
en.method: Leverages the in-context capability of modern diffusion model encoders (LLM/VLM) to let the model act as both teacher and student during training: the student is conditioned only on text features, while the teacher is conditioned on multimodal features of both text and target image; training minimizes two predicted distributions over the student's own roll-outs.
en.result: The model learns new concepts, styles, etc. without sacrificing its original few-step generation capacity.
en.help_to_user: Helps users efficiently fine-tune few-step diffusion models for new tasks or styles while maintaining inference speed.

idea_spark.idea: Combines the in-context capability of LLM/VLM encoders with self-distillation to enable dynamic teacher-student role switching.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the encoder's in-context capability; insufficient encoder capacity may degrade distillation; training computational cost is high.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverages the model's own trajectory for self-supervision, avoiding external teacher networks and reducing dependency on additional annotations.

papers.title: Stage-adaptive audio diffusion modeling
papers.summary: Recent progress in diffusion-based audio generation and restoration has substantially improved performance across heterogeneous conditioning regimes, including text-conditioned audio generation and audio-conditioned super-resolution. However, training audio diffusion models remains computationally expensive, and most existing pipelines still rely on static optimization recipes that treat the relative importance of training signals as fixed throughout learning. In this work, we argue that a major source of inefficiency lies in the evolving balance between semantic acquisition and generation-oriented refinement. Early training places stronger emphasis on acquiring condition-aligned semantic structure and coarse global organization, whereas later training increasingly emphasizes temporal consistency, perceptual fidelity, and fine-detail refinement. To characterize this evolving balance, we introduce a progress-based regime variable derived from the training-time slope of an SSL-space discrepancy, which measures semantic progress during training. Based on this signal, we develop three complementary stage-aware mechanisms: decayed SSL guidance for early semantic bootstrapping, self-adaptive timestep sampling driven by the regime variable, and structure-aware regularization activated from convergent grouped organization in parameter space. We evaluate these mechanisms on text-conditioned audio generation and audio-conditioned super-resolution. Across both settings, the proposed stage-aware strategies improve convergence behavior and yield gains on the primary generation and spectral reconstruction metrics over standard static baselines. These results support the view that efficient audio diffusion training can benefit from treating external guidance, internal organization, and optimization emphasis as stage-dependent components rather than fixed ingredients.

ai.tldr: 提出阶段自适应音频扩散训练方法，通过SSL空间差异度量训练进度，动态调整引导、采样和正则化策略，提升生成质量和收敛效率。
ai.motivation: 现有音频扩散模型训练计算成本高，且采用固定优化策略，忽略了训练早期侧重语义结构、后期侧重细节精化的动态平衡需求。
ai.method: 引入基于SSL空间差异的训练进度变量，设计三种阶段自适应机制：衰减SSL引导、自适应时间步采样、结构感知正则化。
ai.result: 在文本条件音频生成和音频超分辨率任务上，相比静态基线，收敛更快，生成质量和频谱重建指标均有提升。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过动态调整训练策略来提升音频扩散模型的训练效率和生成质量。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将进度变量与强化学习结合，自动学习最优阶段切换策略。
idea_spark.risk: SSL空间差异度量可能对数据分布敏感，需验证跨数据集泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受课程学习启发，将训练过程视为从粗到细的渐进学习，启发其他生成模型训练策略设计。

idea_spark.risk: SSL空间差异度量可能对数据分布敏感，需验证跨数据集泛化性。

en.tldr: Proposes stage-adaptive audio diffusion training that uses SSL-space discrepancy to measure progress and dynamically adjusts guidance, sampling, and regularization, improving generation quality and convergence efficiency.
en.motivation: Existing audio diffusion training is computationally expensive and uses static optimization, ignoring the evolving balance between early semantic acquisition and later fine-detail refinement.
en.method: Introduces a progress variable based on SSL-space discrepancy, and develops three stage-aware mechanisms: decayed SSL guidance, self-adaptive timestep sampling, and structure-aware regularization.
en.result: On text-conditioned audio generation and audio super-resolution, the method achieves faster convergence and gains in primary generation and spectral reconstruction metrics over static baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how dynamically adjusting training strategies can improve efficiency and quality in audio diffusion models.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining the progress variable with reinforcement learning to automatically learn optimal stage-switching policies.
idea_spark.risk: The SSL-space discrepancy may be sensitive to data distribution; cross-dataset generalization needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by curriculum learning, treating training as coarse-to-fine progressive learning, inspiring training strategy design for other generative models.

papers.title: SCOUT: Active Information Foraging for Long-Text Understanding with Decoupled Epistemic States
papers.summary: Long-Text Understanding (LTU) at million-token scale requires balancing reasoning fidelity with computational efficiency. Frontier long-context LLMs can process millions of token contexts end-to-end, but they suffer from high token consumption and attention dilution. In parallel, specialized LTU agents often sacrifice fidelity through task-agnostic abstractions like graph construction or indexing. We identify a key insight for LTU: query-relevant information is typically sparse relative to the full document, so effective reasoning should rely on a query-sufficient subset rather than the entire context. To address this, we propose SCOUT, a new paradigm for LTU that shifts from passive processing to active information foraging. It treats the document as an explorable environment and answers from a compact, provenance-grounded epistemic state. Guided by state-level gap diagnosis, SCOUT adaptively alternates between coarse-to-fine exploration and anchored state updates that progressively contract its epistemic state toward query sufficiency. Experiments show that SCOUT matches state-of-the-art proprietary models while reducing token consumption by up to 8x. Moreover, SCOUT remains stable as context length scales, substantially alleviating the practical cost-performance trade-off.

ai.tldr: 提出SCOUT范式，将长文本理解从被动处理转为主动信息觅食，通过解耦认知状态和自适应探索，在百万token规模下实现高效推理。
ai.motivation: 现有长文本理解方法要么端到端处理导致高token消耗和注意力稀释，要么使用任务无关抽象牺牲保真度。
ai.method: 将文档视为可探索环境，维护紧凑的认知状态，通过状态级差距诊断自适应交替进行粗到细探索和锚定状态更新。
ai.result: 匹配最先进专有模型性能，同时减少最多8倍token消耗，且随上下文长度扩展保持稳定。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效处理超长文档，降低计算成本而不损失推理质量。

idea_spark.idea: 将认知状态解耦为探索和更新两个独立过程，实现自适应信息收集。
idea_spark.risk: 状态级差距诊断可能引入额外计算开销，且对复杂文档的探索策略可能不够鲁棒。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类主动阅读策略启发，先扫读再精读关键部分。

en.tldr: SCOUT proposes a paradigm shift from passive processing to active information foraging for long-text understanding, using decoupled epistemic states and adaptive exploration to achieve efficient reasoning at million-token scale.
en.motivation: Existing long-text understanding methods either process end-to-end with high token consumption and attention dilution, or use task-agnostic abstractions that sacrifice fidelity.
en.method: Treats the document as an explorable environment, maintains a compact epistemic state, and adaptively alternates between coarse-to-fine exploration and anchored state updates guided by state-level gap diagnosis.
en.result: Matches state-of-the-art proprietary models while reducing token consumption by up to 8x, and remains stable as context length scales.
en.help_to_user: Helps users efficiently process extremely long documents, reducing computational cost without compromising reasoning quality.

idea_spark.idea: Decouple the epistemic state into exploration and update processes for adaptive information gathering.
idea_spark.risk: State-level gap diagnosis may introduce additional computational overhead, and exploration strategies may not be robust for complex documents.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human active reading strategies: first skim then read key parts in detail.

papers.title: Uno-Orchestra: Parsimonious Agent Routing via Selective Delegation
papers.summary: Large language model (LLM) multi-agent systems typically rely on rigid orchestration, committing either to flat per-query routing or to hand-engineered task decomposition, so decomposition depth, worker choice, and inference budget are not jointly optimized under one objective. We introduce Uno-Orchestra, a unified orchestration policy that selectively decomposes a task and dispatches each subtask to an admissible (model, primitive) pair, with both decisions learned together from curated RL trajectories grounded in real worker interactions. Against 22 baselines on a 13-benchmark suite spanning math, code, knowledge, long-context, and agentic tool-use, Uno-Orchestra reaches 77.0% macro pass@1, roughly 16% above the strongest workflow baseline, at roughly an order of magnitude lower per-query cost, advancing the accuracy-efficiency frontier of selective delegation.

ai.tldr: 提出Uno-Orchestra，一种统一编排策略，通过强化学习联合学习任务分解和子任务分配，在13个基准测试上以更低成本实现更高准确率。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM多智能体系统依赖固定编排，无法联合优化分解深度、工人选择和推理预算。
ai.method: 从真实交互的RL轨迹中学习统一编排策略，选择性分解任务并分配给合适的（模型，原语）对。
ai.result: 在22个基线上，Uno-Orchestra达到77.0% macro pass@1，比最强工作流基线高约16%，成本低一个数量级。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效选择智能体组合，降低推理成本同时提升任务完成质量。

idea_spark.idea: 将任务分解与工人选择联合优化，可启发设计自适应推理预算分配机制。
idea_spark.risk: RL训练依赖高质量轨迹数据，数据偏差可能导致策略泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受经济学中委托代理理论启发，将智能体路由视为最优契约设计问题。

en.tldr: Uno-Orchestra is a unified orchestration policy that jointly learns task decomposition and subtask assignment via RL, achieving higher accuracy at lower cost across 13 benchmarks.
en.motivation: Existing LLM multi-agent systems rely on rigid orchestration and cannot jointly optimize decomposition depth, worker selection, and inference budget.
en.method: Learn a unified orchestration policy from curated RL trajectories grounded in real interactions, selectively decomposing tasks and dispatching to admissible (model, primitive) pairs.
en.result: Uno-Orchestra achieves 77.0% macro pass@1, roughly 16% above the strongest workflow baseline, at roughly an order of magnitude lower per-query cost.
en.help_to_user: Helps users efficiently select agent combinations, reducing inference cost while improving task completion quality.

idea_spark.idea: Jointly optimizing task decomposition and worker selection can inspire adaptive inference budget allocation mechanisms.
idea_spark.risk: RL training relies on high-quality trajectory data; data bias may lead to insufficient policy generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by principal-agent theory in economics, treating agent routing as an optimal contract design problem.

papers.title: Analysis of a Competitive Bivirus SIS Epidemic Model with Game Theoretic Social Distancing
papers.summary: We propose a competitive bi-virus model with dynamic social distancing behavior. Our model illustrates how public perception of different viruses changes the conditions for their eradication, their coexistence, or the dominance of one over the other. We show that our model is not monotone, in contrast to the classic bi-virus model. We detail how social distancing behavior produces different sets of equilibria than the classic bi-virus model and changes the criteria for their stability. In particular, we detail the set of disease free equilibria (DFE) present in our model and identify necessary and sufficient conditions for almost global exponential stability of the same. We prove similar global results for all but one non-DFE isolated (unilateral) equilibria and local stability results for the remainder. We also consider coexistence equilibria; we show such equilibria, when they exist, take the form of lines of equilibria and give local conditions for their stability. Finally, we illustrate our theoretical findings with numerical examples.

ai.tldr: 提出一个竞争性双病毒SIS流行病模型，结合博弈论社交距离行为，分析病毒根除、共存或主导的条件。
ai.motivation: 经典双病毒模型未考虑公众感知对社交距离行为的影响，而实际中不同病毒的威胁感知会改变传播动态。
ai.method: 构建非单调的竞争性双病毒SIS模型，引入基于博弈论的社交距离决策，通过平衡点分析和稳定性理论（全局指数稳定、局部稳定）推导条件。
ai.result: 模型产生不同于经典模型的平衡点集合，包括疾病自由平衡点（DFE）的几乎全局指数稳定条件、单边平衡点的全局结果以及共存平衡点的线状结构及其局部稳定性条件。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解公众行为如何影响多病毒传播的最终结局，为公共卫生干预策略（如信息宣传、社交距离政策）提供理论依据。

idea_spark.idea: 考虑时变感知或网络结构（如社交网络中的信息扩散）对社交距离行为的影响，扩展模型。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设个体完全理性，实际行为可能受情绪、偏见影响，导致预测偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“公众感知改变平衡点”出发，可设计自适应干预策略，如动态调整社交距离建议以应对不同病毒流行阶段。
