idea_spark.risk: 模型假设个体完全理性，实际行为可能受情绪、偏见影响，导致预测偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“公众感知改变平衡点”出发，可设计自适应干预策略，如动态调整社交距离建议以应对不同病毒流行阶段。

en.tldr: Proposes a competitive bivirus SIS epidemic model with game-theoretic social distancing, analyzing conditions for eradication, coexistence, or dominance of viruses.
en.motivation: Classic bivirus models ignore how public perception of virus threats alters social distancing behavior, which can fundamentally change epidemic outcomes.
en.method: Develops a non-monotone competitive bivirus SIS model incorporating game-theoretic social distancing decisions, using equilibrium analysis and stability theory (global exponential stability, local stability) to derive conditions.
en.result: The model yields equilibrium sets distinct from classic models, including almost global exponential stability of disease-free equilibria, global results for unilateral equilibria, and line-shaped coexistence equilibria with local stability conditions.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how public behavior shapes multi-virus epidemic outcomes, providing theoretical guidance for public health interventions such as information campaigns and social distancing policies.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the model to incorporate time-varying perceptions or network structures (e.g., information diffusion in social networks) affecting social distancing behavior.
idea_spark.risk: Assumes fully rational individuals; actual behavior may be influenced by emotions or biases, leading to prediction errors.
idea_spark.inspiration: From the insight that public perception shifts equilibria, design adaptive intervention strategies, such as dynamically adjusting social distancing recommendations based on the current dominant virus.

papers.title: Lightweight Vulnerability Detection from Code Metrics and Token Features
papers.summary: Vulnerability detection for C/C++ code increasingly relies on heavy representations such as code graphs and deep models, while many practical workflows still benefit from fast and reproducible ranking baselines for human triage. This preprint studies a lightweight function-level vulnerability triage pipeline that combines sparse token n-grams from raw function text with a small set of inexpensive code metrics, including NLOC, approximate cyclomatic complexity, token count, maximum brace depth, and parameter count. We use TF-IDF token features and a class-weighted logistic regression classifier, avoiding deep learning, transformers, and program graphs.   Using the Devign function-level labels, we evaluate random and cross-project settings, including a FFmpeg-to-QEMU transfer experiment. We emphasize precision-recall AUC and Recall@10% as ranking-oriented metrics for skewed or triage-oriented workloads. On the random split, the best combined variant reaches PR-AUC 0.642 and Recall@10% 0.161, while cross-project generalization is substantially harder, with PR-AUC around 0.436. We further report ablations, test-only identifier-renaming robustness, and end-to-end efficiency. The results suggest that simple token and metric features provide a useful transparent baseline, but also expose sensitivity to superficial lexical cues and limited cross-project transfer.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了一种轻量级的C/C++代码漏洞分类流水线，结合稀疏token n-gram和少量代码度量，使用TF-IDF和逻辑回归，在Devign数据集上评估了随机和跨项目设置。
ai.motivation: 现有漏洞检测方法依赖深度模型和图表示，计算成本高，而实际工作流需要快速、可复现的排序基线。
ai.method: 提取函数文本的稀疏token n-gram和代码度量（如NLOC、圈复杂度、token数、最大括号深度、参数数），使用TF-IDF特征和类加权逻辑回归分类器。
ai.result: 随机划分下最佳组合PR-AUC为0.642，Recall@10%为0.161；跨项目泛化困难，PR-AUC约0.436。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个透明、高效的漏洞分类基线，适合人工分类的快速排序。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将token特征与代码度量结合，用于跨项目漏洞检测的迁移学习。
idea_spark.risk: 模型对表面词汇线索敏感，跨项目泛化能力有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 简单特征和模型可作为复杂方法的基线，并揭示数据偏差问题。

en.motivation: Existing vulnerability detection methods rely on heavy representations like code graphs and deep models, while practical workflows need fast and reproducible ranking baselines.
en.method: Extract sparse token n-grams from raw function text and a small set of code metrics (NLOC, approximate cyclomatic complexity, token count, max brace depth, parameter count), using TF-IDF features and a class-weighted logistic regression classifier.

papers.title: Temporal Reasoning Is Not the Bottleneck: A Probabilistic Inconsistency Framework for Neuro-Symbolic QA
papers.summary: Despite significant advances, large language models (LLMs) continue to exhibit brittle performance on complex temporal reasoning tasks. This failure mode is widely attributed to inherent deficits in autoregressive logical deduction. In this paper, we challenge this prevailing narrative, demonstrating that temporal reasoning is not the fundamental bottleneck; rather, the locus of failure lies in unstructured text-to-event representation. We introduce a novel neuro-symbolic question-answering framework governed by a Probabilistic Inconsistency Signal (PIS) that explicitly isolates perceptual errors from reasoning failures. By lifting unstructured text into explicit event graphs and interval constraints, our architecture strictly decouples semantic extraction from a symbolic reasoning engine. To robustly detect structural breaks, the PIS elegantly unifies symbolic credal intervals with epistemic neural uncertainty extracted via Evidential Deep Learning on LLM hidden states. Empirical evaluations reveal a striking paradigm shift: when provided with correct structural representations, our system's explicit proof traces achieve perfect 1.0 accuracy (4000/4000) and strictly zero false positives/negatives on temporal arithmetic benchmarks. On broader, noise-injected QA settings, the framework maintains a competitive 75.1\% accuracy while enabling deterministic, step-level failure localization. Ultimately, by isolating the representation bottleneck from the reasoning substrate, this work reframes temporal QA from an algorithmic reasoning challenge to a structural alignment problem, charting a verifiable path forward for reliable neuro-symbolic AI.

ai.tldr: 本文挑战了大型语言模型在时间推理任务中表现脆弱的常见归因，提出时间推理并非瓶颈，而是非结构化文本到事件表示的转换存在问题。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM在复杂时间推理上表现不佳，通常被归因于自回归逻辑推理缺陷，但作者认为真正的瓶颈在于文本到事件的结构化表示。
ai.method: 提出概率不一致信号（PIS）框架，将非结构化文本提升为显式事件图和区间约束，通过符号推理引擎解耦语义提取，并融合符号信度区间与基于证据深度学习的不确定性。
ai.result: 在正确结构表示下，时间算术基准达到100%准确率（4000/4000），零假阳/假阴；在噪声注入QA设置中保持75.1%准确率，并支持确定性步骤级故障定位。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解时间问答任务中表示瓶颈与推理瓶颈的区别，并提供一种可验证的神经符号方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将PIS与多模态输入结合，处理视频或图像中的时间事件表示。
idea_spark.risk: 事件图构建可能依赖领域知识，泛化到开放域时表示质量可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用符号不确定性信号指导神经网络的表示学习，可能提升模型在分布外场景下的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 将PIS与多模态输入结合，处理视频或图像中的时间事件表示。
idea_spark.risk: 事件图构建可能依赖领域知识，泛化到开放域时表示质量可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用符号不确定性信号指导神经网络的表示学习，可能提升模型在分布外场景下的鲁棒性。

en.tldr: This paper challenges the common attribution of LLM brittleness in temporal reasoning, showing that the bottleneck is not reasoning but unstructured text-to-event representation.
en.motivation: LLMs perform poorly on complex temporal reasoning, often blamed on autoregressive logical deduction deficits, but the authors argue the real bottleneck is structural representation from text.
en.method: A Probabilistic Inconsistency Signal (PIS) framework lifts unstructured text into explicit event graphs and interval constraints, decoupling semantic extraction from a symbolic reasoning engine, and unifies symbolic credal intervals with epistemic neural uncertainty from Evidential Deep Learning.
en.result: With correct structural representations, temporal arithmetic benchmarks achieve perfect 1.0 accuracy (4000/4000) with zero false positives/negatives; on noise-injected QA, 75.1% accuracy with deterministic step-level failure localization.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the distinction between representation and reasoning bottlenecks in temporal QA, and provides a verifiable neuro-symbolic approach.

idea_spark.idea: Combine PIS with multimodal inputs to handle temporal event representation in videos or images.
idea_spark.risk: Event graph construction may rely on domain knowledge, potentially degrading representation quality in open domains.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using symbolic uncertainty signals to guide neural representation learning may improve model robustness under distribution shifts.

papers.title: UFAL-CUNI at SemEval-2026 Task 11: An Efficient Modular Neuro-symbolic Method for Syllogistic Reasoning
papers.summary: This paper describes our system submitted to SemEval-2026 Task 11: Disentangling Content and Formal Reasoning in Large Language Models. We present an efficient modular neuro-symbolic approach, combining a symbolic prover with small reasoning LLMs (4B parameters). The system consists of an LLM-based parser that translates natural language syllogisms to a first-order logic (FOL) representation, an automated theorem prover, and two optional modules: machine translation for multilingual inputs and a symbolic retrieval component for the identification of relevant premises. The system achieves competitive accuracy and relatively low content effect on most subtasks. Our ablations show that this approach outperforms LLM-based zero-shot baselines in this parameter size range, but also reveal limited multilingual capabilities of small LLMs. Finally, we include a discussion of the task's main ranking metric and analyze its limitations.

ai.tldr: 提出一种高效的模块化神经符号方法，结合符号证明器和小型推理LLM（4B参数），用于三段论推理任务。
ai.motivation: 解决大型语言模型在形式推理中内容与形式推理的纠缠问题，提升推理准确性和多语言能力。
ai.method: 系统包括基于LLM的解析器（将自然语言三段论转化为一阶逻辑）、自动定理证明器，以及可选的机器翻译和符号检索模块。
ai.result: 在大多数子任务上取得竞争性准确率和较低的内容效应，优于同参数规模的零样本基线，但小型LLM的多语言能力有限。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可解释、模块化的推理框架，适用于需要严格逻辑推理的场景，如法律、科学论证。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号检索模块扩展为动态知识库，结合外部结构化知识提升推理鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 符号证明器可能无法处理模糊或非单调逻辑，且多语言解析质量依赖LLM的翻译能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴神经符号方法在形式推理中的优势，探索如何在小参数模型上实现高效推理。

en.tldr: An efficient modular neuro-symbolic method combining a symbolic prover with small reasoning LLMs (4B parameters) for syllogistic reasoning.
en.motivation: To disentangle content and formal reasoning in LLMs, improving accuracy and multilingual capability for syllogistic tasks.
en.method: The system includes an LLM-based parser converting natural language syllogisms to first-order logic, an automated theorem prover, and optional machine translation and symbolic retrieval modules.
en.result: Achieves competitive accuracy and low content effect on most subtasks, outperforming zero-shot baselines of similar size, but reveals limited multilingual abilities of small LLMs.
en.help_to_user: Provides an interpretable, modular reasoning framework suitable for tasks requiring strict logical inference, such as legal or scientific argumentation.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the symbolic retrieval module into a dynamic knowledge base, incorporating external structured knowledge to improve reasoning robustness.
idea_spark.risk: The symbolic prover may struggle with ambiguous or non-monotonic logic, and multilingual parsing quality depends on LLM translation capability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage the strengths of neuro-symbolic methods in formal reasoning to explore efficient inference on small-parameter models.

papers.title: OPENJ: A Conceptual Framework for Open-Source Digital Human Modeling and Ergonomic Assessment in a CAD Environment
papers.summary: Industrial workplace challenges range from musculoskeletal disorders -- a leading cause of occupational injury -- to suboptimal workstation layouts, inefficient task sequences, and poor human-equipment fit. Digital human modeling (DHM) tools address several of these challenges by placing a scalable virtual mannequin in a computer-aided design (CAD) environment, enabling engineers to evaluate ergonomic risk through standardized assessment methods (RULA, REBA, NIOSH Lifting Equation, OWAS), optimize workstation layouts for reach and visibility, predict task postures through inverse kinematics, and simulate operations before physical implementation. Despite four decades of development since the Jack system originated at the University of Pennsylvania in the 1980s, the integrated DHM capability set -- anthropometric mannequin, posture prediction, ergonomic assessment, and CAD integration -- remains exclusive to commercial platforms such as Siemens Tecnomatix Jack (Process Simulate), Dassault DELMIA, Humanetics RAMSIS, and the University of Iowa's Santos system. These platforms operate under proprietary, vendor-quoted pricing models, and their acquisition and operating costs, together with closed-source implementations, have been repeatedly identified as practical adoption barriers for individual researchers, small-to-medium enterprises, and educational institutions. Organizations without access resort to manual observational methods -- paper-based worksheets applied to photographs or video -- sacrificing the predictive power and reproducibility that computational analysis provides. The paper serves as a design blueprint for (OpenJane/Joe), positioning the project for subsequent open-source implementation and community adoption.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一个名为OPENJ的开源数字人体建模与工效学评估框架的设计蓝图，旨在解决现有商业DHM工具成本高、封闭的问题。
ai.motivation: 现有商业数字人体建模工具（如Jack、DELMIA）成本高且闭源，阻碍了研究人员、中小企业和教育机构的使用。
ai.method: 设计了一个集成人体测量模型、姿态预测、工效学评估（RULA、REBA等）和CAD环境的开源框架蓝图。
ai.result: 提出了OpenJane/Joe项目的设计蓝图，为后续开源实现和社区采用奠定基础。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何构建一个低成本、可访问的数字人体建模与工效学评估工具。

idea_spark.idea: 基于该蓝图，可开发一个集成多种工效学评估方法的开源插件，直接嵌入主流CAD软件。
idea_spark.risk: 开源实现可能面临维护不足、与商业软件兼容性差的风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴开源社区的成功模式（如Blender），推动工效学工具的民主化。

en.tldr: This paper presents a design blueprint for OPENJ, an open-source digital human modeling and ergonomic assessment framework, aiming to address the high cost and closed-source nature of existing commercial DHM tools.
en.motivation: Existing commercial digital human modeling tools (e.g., Jack, DELMIA) are expensive and closed-source, hindering adoption by researchers, SMEs, and educational institutions.
en.method: Designed a blueprint for an open-source framework integrating anthropometric mannequin, posture prediction, ergonomic assessment (RULA, REBA, etc.), and CAD environment.
en.result: Proposed a design blueprint for the OpenJane/Joe project, laying the groundwork for subsequent open-source implementation and community adoption.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to build a low-cost, accessible digital human modeling and ergonomic assessment tool.

idea_spark.idea: Based on this blueprint, develop an open-source plugin integrating multiple ergonomic assessment methods that can be embedded directly into mainstream CAD software.
idea_spark.risk: Open-source implementation may face risks of insufficient maintenance and poor compatibility with commercial software.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from successful open-source community models (e.g., Blender) to democratize ergonomic tools.

papers.title: Can the $L^1$-$L^\infty$ duality be restored for non-dominated families of probability measures?
papers.summary: The duality $L^{\infty}\simeq (L^{1})'$ frequently breaks down in the presence of model uncertainty, where a single reference measure $P$ is replaced by a non-dominated family of probability measures $\mathcal{P}$. The unavailability of classical measure-theoretic and functional-analytic tools in this regime poses a significant obstacle to developing robust probabilistic frameworks. We show that this duality can be restored for a broad class of robust statistical models by extending the underlying probability space. Specifically, on the extended model, the space $\mathbb{L}^{\infty}(\mathcal{P})$ of $\mathcal{P}$-quasi-surely bounded functions is isometrically isomorphic to the dual of the space of finite signed measures absolutely continuous with respect to at least one element of $\mathcal{P}$. The proposed extension is canonical: it is the smallest $\mathcal{P}$-complete extension of the original $σ$-algebra for which $\mathbb{L}^{\infty}(\mathcal{P})$ is the dual of any normed space. Our assumptions encompass several prominent non-dominated settings, including infinite product measures, Gaussian processes, the Black-Scholes model with uncertain constant volatility and drift, robust binomial models, and, more generally, infinite sequences from any parametric model with almost surely estimable parameters. Furthermore, we unify the existing frameworks of Cohen (2012) and Liebrich et al. (2022), demonstrating that our construction is equivalent to the capacity-based approach under mild assumptions satisfied by the aforementioned examples. Finally, we apply our theory to extend Kraft's (1955) characterization of strictly unbiased hypothesis tests to non-dominated cases.

ai.tldr: 本文证明，通过扩展概率空间，可以在非支配概率测度族中恢复L^1-L^∞对偶性，从而统一了现有框架并推广了Kraft的严格无偏假设检验。
ai.motivation: 经典L^∞≃(L^1)'对偶性在模型不确定性下失效，阻碍了稳健概率框架的发展。
ai.method: 通过构造最小的P-完备σ-代数扩展，使L^∞(P)等距同构于某个有限符号测度空间的对偶。
ai.result: 该扩展是典范的，适用于多种非支配设定（如无限乘积测度、高斯过程、Black-Scholes模型等），并统一了Cohen和Liebrich等人的方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理模型不确定性下的对偶性问题提供了理论工具，可应用于稳健统计和金融数学。

idea_spark.idea: 利用最小完备扩展恢复对偶性，或许能用于解决分布鲁棒优化中的对偶间隙问题。
idea_spark.risk: 扩展后的空间可能过于抽象，实际计算或近似时需谨慎。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Kraft检验的推广启发，可探索其他经典统计结果在非支配框架下的鲁棒版本。

idea_spark.risk: 扩展后的空间可能过于抽象，实际计算或近似时需谨慎。

en.tldr: This paper shows that by extending the probability space, the L^1-L^∞ duality can be restored for non-dominated families of probability measures, unifying existing frameworks and extending Kraft's characterization of strictly unbiased hypothesis tests.
en.motivation: The classical L^∞≃(L^1)' duality breaks down under model uncertainty, hindering the development of robust probabilistic frameworks.
en.method: A canonical minimal P-complete extension of the σ-algebra is constructed, making L^∞(P) isometrically isomorphic to the dual of a space of finite signed measures.
en.result: The extension applies to a broad class of non-dominated settings (e.g., infinite product measures, Gaussian processes, Black-Scholes with uncertain volatility) and unifies the approaches of Cohen and Liebrich et al.
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical tools for duality problems under model uncertainty, applicable to robust statistics and mathematical finance.

idea_spark.idea: Restoring duality via minimal complete extensions might resolve duality gaps in distributionally robust optimization.
idea_spark.risk: The extended space may be too abstract for practical computation or approximation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the extension of Kraft's test, one could explore robust versions of other classical statistical results under non-dominated families.

papers.title: Reduced-order Neural Modeling with Differentiable Simulation for High-Detail Tactile Perception
papers.summary: Tactile perception is key to dexterous manipulation, yet simulating high-resolution elastomer deformation remains computationally prohibitive. Finite element methods (FEM) deliver high fidelity but demand costly remeshing, while Material Point Methods (MPM) suffer from heavy particle-memory tradeoffs. We propose a {reduced-order neural simulation framework} that couples coarse-grained MPM dynamics with an implicit neural decoder to reconstruct sub-particle tactile details from compact latent states. The framework learns a continuous deformation manifold from paired high- and low-resolution simulations, enabling physically consistent, differentiable inference. Compared to the TacIPC, our method achieves over 65\% faster simulation and {40\% lower memory usage}, while maintaining better geometric fidelity. In tactile rendering and 3D surface reconstruction, our methods further improve accuracy by 25\% and produce realistic depth images and surface mesh within a faster inference speed. These results demonstrate that the proposed reduced-order neural model enables high-detail, physically grounded tactile simulation with substantial efficiency gains for robotic interaction and optimization.

ai.tldr: 提出一种降阶神经模拟框架，结合粗粒度MPM与隐式神经解码器，实现高细节触觉感知的高效物理仿真。
ai.motivation: 高分辨率弹性体变形仿真计算成本高，传统FEM和MPM方法存在重网格或内存开销大的问题。
ai.method: 使用粗粒度MPM模拟宏观动力学，通过隐式神经解码器从紧凑隐状态重建亚粒子触觉细节，学习连续变形流形。
ai.result: 相比TacIPC，仿真速度提升65%以上，内存降低40%，几何保真度更好；触觉渲染和3D重建精度提高25%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速理解该降阶神经模拟方法在触觉感知中的效率与精度优势。

idea_spark.idea: 将粗粒度物理模拟与隐式神经解码结合，实现高效且可微的触觉仿真。
idea_spark.risk: 神经解码器可能对训练数据分布敏感，泛化到新几何或材料时需重新训练。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受神经辐射场（NeRF）隐式表示启发，将连续变形流形学习用于触觉仿真。

en.tldr: A reduced-order neural simulation framework combining coarse-grained MPM with an implicit neural decoder for efficient high-detail tactile perception.
en.motivation: High-resolution elastomer deformation simulation is computationally prohibitive; traditional FEM and MPM suffer from remeshing or memory overhead.
en.method: Coarse-grained MPM captures macroscopic dynamics, while an implicit neural decoder reconstructs sub-particle tactile details from compact latent states, learning a continuous deformation manifold.
en.result: Compared to TacIPC, 65% faster simulation, 40% lower memory usage, better geometric fidelity; 25% improvement in tactile rendering and 3D reconstruction accuracy.
en.help_to_user: Helps users quickly grasp the efficiency and accuracy advantages of this reduced-order neural simulation method for tactile perception.

idea_spark.idea: Combine coarse-grained physics simulation with an implicit neural decoder for efficient and differentiable tactile simulation.
idea_spark.risk: The neural decoder may be sensitive to training data distribution, requiring retraining for new geometries or materials.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by neural radiance fields (NeRF) for implicit representation, applied to learning continuous deformation manifolds for tactile simulation.

papers.title: From Pixels to Tokens: A Systematic Study of Latent Action Supervision for Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.summary: Latent actions serve as an intermediate representation that enables consistent modeling of vision-language-action (VLA) models across heterogeneous datasets. However, approaches to supervising VLAs with latent actions are fragmented and lack a systematic comparison. This work structures the study of latent action supervision from two perspectives: (i) regularizing the trajectory via image-based latent actions, and (ii) unifying the target space with action-based latent actions. Under a unified VLA baseline, we instantiate and compare four representative integration strategies. Our results reveal a formulation-task correspondence: image-based latent actions benefit long-horizon reasoning and scene-level generalization, whereas action-based latent actions excel at complex motor coordination. Furthermore, we find that directly supervising the VLM with discrete latent action tokens yields the most effective performance. Finally, our experiments offer initial insights into the benefits of latent action supervision in mixed-data, suggesting a promising direction for VLA training. Code is available at https://github.com/RUCKBReasoning/From_Pixels_to_Tokens.

ai.tldr: 系统研究了视觉-语言-动作模型中潜在动作监督的两种策略（基于图像和基于动作），发现离散潜在动作令牌直接监督效果最佳，且不同策略适用于不同任务。
ai.motivation: 现有潜在动作监督方法碎片化，缺乏系统比较，需要统一框架来指导VLA模型训练。
ai.method: 在统一VLA基线框架下，实例化并比较了四种代表性集成策略，分别从轨迹正则化和目标空间统一两个角度进行实验。
ai.result: 基于图像的潜在动作有利于长程推理和场景泛化，基于动作的潜在动作擅长复杂运动协调；直接使用离散潜在动作令牌监督VLM效果最优。
ai.help_to_user: 为VLA模型选择潜在动作监督策略提供了明确指导，帮助用户根据任务需求（长程推理 vs. 精细控制）选择合适方法。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将离散潜在动作令牌与强化学习中的动作空间结合，实现从演示到自主策略的平滑迁移。
idea_spark.risk: 离散化可能丢失连续动作的细微信息，在需要高精度控制的任务中可能性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 混合数据训练中潜在动作监督的初步优势提示：未来可设计自适应策略，根据任务动态切换或融合不同潜在动作表示。

zh.tldr: 系统研究了视觉-语言-动作模型中潜在动作监督的两种策略（基于图像和基于动作），发现离散潜在动作令牌直接监督效果最佳，且不同策略适用于不同任务。
zh.motivation: 现有潜在动作监督方法碎片化，缺乏系统比较，需要统一框架来指导VLA模型训练。
zh.method: 在统一VLA基线框架下，实例化并比较了四种代表性集成策略，分别从轨迹正则化和目标空间统一两个角度进行实验。
zh.result: 基于图像的潜在动作有利于长程推理和场景泛化，基于动作的潜在动作擅长复杂运动协调；直接使用离散潜在动作令牌监督VLM效果最优。
zh.help_to_user: 为VLA模型选择潜在动作监督策略提供了明确指导，帮助用户根据任务需求（长程推理 vs. 精细控制）选择合适方法。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将离散潜在动作令牌与强化学习中的动作空间结合，实现从演示到自主策略的平滑迁移。
idea_spark.risk: 离散化可能丢失连续动作的细微信息，在需要高精度控制的任务中可能性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 混合数据训练中潜在动作监督的初步优势提示：未来可设计自适应策略，根据任务动态切换或融合不同潜在动作表示。

en.tldr: A systematic study of latent action supervision in VLA models, comparing image-based and action-based strategies, finding discrete latent action tokens most effective and task-dependent benefits.
en.motivation: Existing approaches to latent action supervision are fragmented; a unified comparison is needed to guide VLA training.
en.method: Under a unified VLA baseline, four integration strategies are instantiated and compared, covering trajectory regularization and target space unification.
en.result: Image-based latent actions benefit long-horizon reasoning and scene generalization; action-based latent actions excel at complex motor coordination; direct supervision with discrete latent action tokens yields best performance.
en.help_to_user: Provides clear guidance on selecting latent action supervision strategies for VLA models based on task requirements (long-horizon reasoning vs. fine-grained control).

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining discrete latent action tokens with reinforcement learning action spaces for smooth transfer from demonstration to autonomous policy.
idea_spark.risk: Discretization may lose fine-grained continuous action information, potentially degrading performance in high-precision control tasks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Preliminary benefits in mixed-data training suggest designing adaptive strategies that dynamically switch or fuse different latent action representations based on the task.

papers.title: Few-Shot Learning Pipeline for Monkeypox Skin Disease Classification Using CNN Feature Extractors
papers.summary: Despite the strong performance of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) in disease classification, their effectiveness often depends on access to large annotated datasets, which is an impractical requirement for emerging or rare conditions such as Monkeypox. To overcome this limitation, we propose a few-shot learning (FSL) framework that employs SimpleShot, a lightweight, non-parametric, inductive classifier, for Monkeypox and pox-like skin disease recognition from limited labeled examples. The proposed pipeline passes the skin lesion images through a frozen, pretrained CNN backbone to obtain feature embeddings, which are then classified via SimpleShot using nearest-centroid comparisons in a normalized embedding space. We systematically benchmark six widely used CNN backbones as feature extractors under consistent experimental settings, enabling fair comparison. Experiments on three publicly available datasets (MSLD v1.0, MSID, and MSLD v2.0) are conducted across 2-way, 4-way, and 6-way tasks with 1-shot, 5-shot, and 10-shot configurations. Among all models, MobileNetV2_100 consistently achieves the highest accuracy. In addition, we present a cross-dataset evaluation for Monkeypox classification, revealing that binary Mpox-vs-Others transfer remains comparatively stable while multi-class performance degrades significantly under domain shift. Together, these results demonstrate the practical utility of combining inductive FSL methods with lightweight CNN backbones and highlight the importance of domain robustness for reliable real-world clinical deployment.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于SimpleShot的少样本学习流水线，利用预训练CNN提取特征，在猴痘皮肤病变分类中实现高精度，并评估跨数据集泛化能力。
ai.motivation: CNN在疾病分类中依赖大规模标注数据，但猴痘等罕见病难以获取充足样本，需要少样本学习方法。
ai.method: 使用冻结的预训练CNN骨干提取特征嵌入，通过SimpleShot在归一化嵌入空间中进行最近质心分类，并在2/4/6-way和1/5/10-shot设置下系统比较六种CNN骨干。
ai.result: MobileNetV2_100在所有配置下准确率最高；二分类跨数据集迁移稳定，但多分类性能因域偏移显著下降。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种轻量级、非参数的少样本分类流水线，可直接用于类似罕见皮肤病的快速诊断系统开发。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在嵌入空间中引入对比学习或原型网络变体，以进一步提升少样本分类的域鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 跨数据集多分类性能下降明显，实际部署时需考虑域适应或数据增强策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用冻结骨干+简单分类器实现高效少样本学习，启发在资源受限场景下设计更简洁的医学AI系统。

zh.motivation: CNN在疾病分类中依赖大规模标注数据，但猴痘等罕见病难以获取充足样本，需要少样本学习方法。
zh.method: 使用冻结的预训练CNN骨干提取特征嵌入，通过SimpleShot在归一化嵌入空间中进行最近质心分类，并在2/4/6-way和1/5/10-shot设置下系统比较六种CNN骨干。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在嵌入空间中引入对比学习或原型网络变体，以进一步提升少样本分类的域鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 跨数据集多分类性能下降明显，实际部署时需考虑域适应或数据增强策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用冻结骨干+简单分类器实现高效少样本学习，启发在资源受限场景下设计更简洁的医学AI系统。

en.tldr: Proposes a few-shot learning pipeline using SimpleShot with frozen pretrained CNN backbones for Monkeypox skin disease classification, achieving high accuracy and evaluating cross-dataset generalization.
en.motivation: CNNs require large annotated datasets, which are impractical for rare diseases like Monkeypox; few-shot learning is needed.
en.method: Uses frozen pretrained CNN backbones to extract feature embeddings, then classifies via SimpleShot with nearest-centroid comparison in normalized space; benchmarks six backbones under 2/4/6-way and 1/5/10-shot settings.
en.result: MobileNetV2_100 achieves highest accuracy consistently; binary cross-dataset transfer is stable, but multi-class performance degrades significantly under domain shift.
en.help_to_user: Provides a lightweight, non-parametric few-shot classification pipeline that can be directly applied to rapid diagnosis of similar rare skin diseases.

idea_spark.idea: Explore incorporating contrastive learning or prototypical network variants in the embedding space to further improve domain robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Multi-class cross-dataset performance drops significantly; domain adaptation or data augmentation strategies are needed for real-world deployment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using frozen backbones with simple classifiers for efficient few-shot learning inspires designing more concise medical AI systems in resource-constrained settings.

papers.title: Storage Is Not Memory: A Retrieval-Centered Architecture for Agent Recall
papers.summary: Extraction at ingestion is the wrong primitive for agent memory: content discarded before the query is known cannot be recovered at retrieval time. We propose True Memory, a six-layer architecture that shifts the center of the system from a storage schema to a multi-stage retrieval pipeline operating over events preserved verbatim. The full system runs as a single SQLite file on commodity CPU with no external database, vector index, graph store, or GPU. On LoCoMo (1,540 questions across 10 multi-session conversations), True Memory Pro reaches 93.0% accuracy (3-run mean) against 61.4% for Mem0, 65.4% for Supermemory, approximately 71% for Zep, and 94.5% for EverMemOS under a matched gpt-4.1-mini answer model. On LongMemEval (500 questions), True Memory Pro reaches 87.8% (3-run mean). On BEAM-1M (700 questions at the 1-million-token scale), True Memory Pro reaches 76.6% (3-run mean), above the prior published result of 73.9% for Hindsight. A 56-configuration ablation shows a 1.3-percentage-point spread within the top-performing configuration family.

ai.tldr: 提出True Memory架构，将智能体记忆系统从存储模式转向多阶段检索管道，在多个基准上超越现有方法。
ai.motivation: 现有智能体记忆系统在提取时丢弃内容，导致查询时无法恢复，需要更有效的检索中心架构。
ai.method: 设计六层True Memory架构，保留事件原文，通过多阶段检索管道运行在单个SQLite文件中，无需外部数据库或GPU。
ai.result: 在LoCoMo上准确率93.0%，LongMemEval上87.8%，BEAM-1M上76.6%，均优于Mem0、Supermemory等基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何构建高效、轻量的智能体记忆系统，避免存储优先的陷阱。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将事件原文保留与动态查询感知的检索策略结合，提升记忆召回率。
idea_spark.risk: 对超长对话或实时性要求高的场景，检索延迟可能成为瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受数据库查询优化启发，将记忆系统视为检索问题而非存储问题。

idea_spark.risk: 对超长对话或实时性要求高的场景，检索延迟可能成为瓶颈。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受数据库查询优化启发，将记忆系统视为检索问题而非存储问题。

en.tldr: Proposes True Memory, a retrieval-centered architecture that shifts agent memory from storage schema to multi-stage retrieval pipeline, outperforming existing methods on multiple benchmarks.
en.motivation: Existing agent memory systems discard content at ingestion, making it unrecoverable at query time; a retrieval-centered architecture is needed.
en.method: Designs a six-layer True Memory architecture that preserves events verbatim and runs a multi-stage retrieval pipeline in a single SQLite file, requiring no external database or GPU.
en.result: Achieves 93.0% accuracy on LoCoMo, 87.8% on LongMemEval, and 76.6% on BEAM-1M, surpassing Mem0, Supermemory, and other baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to build efficient, lightweight agent memory systems by avoiding storage-first pitfalls.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining verbatim event preservation with dynamic query-aware retrieval strategies to improve recall.
idea_spark.risk: Retrieval latency may become a bottleneck for ultra-long conversations or real-time applications.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by database query optimization, treating memory as a retrieval problem rather than a storage problem.

papers.title: Disentangled Learning Improves Implicit Neural Representations for Medical Reconstruction
papers.summary: Implicit neural representations (INRs) have emerged as a powerful paradigm for medical imaging via physics-informed unsupervised learning. Classical INRs optimize an entire network from scratch for each subject, leading to inefficient training and suboptimal imaging quality. Recent initialization-based approaches attempt to inject population priors into pre-trained networks, yet they rely on high-quality images and often suffer from catastrophic forgetting during fine-tuning. We present DisINR, a novel INR framework that explicitly disentangles shared and subject-specific representations. DisINR introduces a shared encoder-decoder pair and subject-specific encoders, whose features are jointly decoded for image reconstruction. By integrating differentiable forward models, it pre-trains the shared modules directly from limited raw measurements, removing the need for pre-acquired high-quality images. During test-time adaptation, only the subject-specific encoder is optimized, while the shared pair remains frozen, effectively preserving learned priors. Extensive evaluations on three representative medical imaging tasks show that DisINR significantly outperforms state-of-the-art INRs in both reconstruction accuracy and efficiency.

ai.tldr: 提出DisINR框架，通过解耦共享表示与个体表示，提升医学图像重建的精度与效率。
ai.motivation: 传统隐式神经表示（INR）逐主体从头训练，效率低且质量次优；现有初始化方法依赖高质量图像且微调时易遗忘。
ai.method: 设计共享编码器-解码器对与主体特定编码器，联合解码重建；利用可微前向模型从原始测量数据预训练共享模块，测试时仅优化主体特定编码器。
ai.result: 在三个代表性医学成像任务上，重建精度与效率均显著优于现有INR方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需高质量预训练图像、可快速适应新主体的医学图像重建方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将共享编码器替换为轻量级超网络，以生成主体特定编码器的初始参数，进一步加速适应。
idea_spark.risk: 若共享表示与个体表示耦合过强，可能限制个体编码器的表达能力，导致欠拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴元学习中的快速适应策略，将主体特定编码器视为任务特定模块，实现少样本重建。

zh.tldr: 提出DisINR框架，通过解耦共享表示与个体表示，提升医学图像重建的精度与效率。
zh.motivation: 传统隐式神经表示（INR）逐主体从头训练，效率低且质量次优；现有初始化方法依赖高质量图像且微调时易遗忘。

zh.result: 在三个代表性医学成像任务上，重建精度与效率均显著优于现有INR方法。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种无需高质量预训练图像、可快速适应新主体的医学图像重建方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将共享编码器替换为轻量级超网络，以生成主体特定编码器的初始参数，进一步加速适应。
idea_spark.risk: 若共享表示与个体表示耦合过强，可能限制个体编码器的表达能力，导致欠拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴元学习中的快速适应策略，将主体特定编码器视为任务特定模块，实现少样本重建。

en.tldr: Proposes DisINR, a framework that disentangles shared and subject-specific representations to improve accuracy and efficiency of medical image reconstruction.
en.motivation: Classical INRs train from scratch per subject, causing inefficiency and suboptimal quality; existing initialization methods rely on high-quality images and suffer from catastrophic forgetting during fine-tuning.
en.method: Introduces a shared encoder-decoder pair and subject-specific encoders, jointly decoded for reconstruction; pre-trains shared modules directly from raw measurements using differentiable forward models, and only optimizes the subject-specific encoder at test time.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art INRs in both reconstruction accuracy and efficiency on three representative medical imaging tasks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a medical image reconstruction approach that does not require pre-acquired high-quality images and can quickly adapt to new subjects.

idea_spark.idea: Replace the shared encoder with a lightweight hypernetwork to generate initial parameters for the subject-specific encoder, further accelerating adaptation.
idea_spark.risk: If shared and subject-specific representations are too tightly coupled, the subject-specific encoder may lack expressiveness, leading to underfitting.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow fast adaptation strategies from meta-learning, treating the subject-specific encoder as a task-specific module to enable few-shot reconstruction.

papers.title: Exploring Clustering Capability of Inpainting Model Embeddings for Pattern-based Individual Identification
papers.summary: In this paper, we explore deep learning techniques for individual identification of animals based on their skin patterns. Individual identification is crucial in biodiversity monitoring, since it enables analysis of decline or growth of populations, or intra-species interactions within populations. Models trained for the task of individual identification often do not focus on the skin pattern of animals, but on background details or body shape details. These characteristics are not individually specific, or can change drastically through time. We focus on techniques that will make machine learning models more responsive to skin pattern structure when extracting individual visual embeddings from images. For this, we explore image inpainting of task-specific masks as an auxiliary task to enhance ML-based individual identification from animal skin patterns. We propose a comparative analysis among four models as an encoder backbone for the individual identification task. We focus on the case study of zebrafish, which is a widely recognized biological model organism, and which exhibits individually identifying skin patterns. To evaluate encoder backbone performance, we present standard metrics for classification accuracy, embedding clustering metrics, and GradCAM visualizations.

ai.tldr: 探索利用图像修复模型嵌入的聚类能力，实现基于斑马鱼皮肤图案的个体识别。
ai.motivation: 现有动物个体识别模型常关注背景或体型，而非个体特异的皮肤图案，导致识别不准确。
ai.method: 将图像修复作为辅助任务，比较四种编码器骨干网络，结合分类、聚类指标和GradCAM可视化评估。
ai.result: 图像修复辅助任务提升了编码器对皮肤图案结构的敏感性，聚类指标和可视化结果验证了有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种增强模型关注皮肤图案的辅助训练方法，适用于图案化动物的个体识别。

idea_spark.idea: 将图像修复任务与对比学习结合，进一步强化嵌入的判别性。
idea_spark.risk: 修复任务可能引入噪声，需谨慎设计掩码策略以避免过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用自监督辅助任务引导模型关注关键特征，可推广至其他细粒度识别问题。

zh.tldr: 探索利用图像修复模型嵌入的聚类能力，实现基于斑马鱼皮肤图案的个体识别。
zh.motivation: 现有动物个体识别模型常关注背景或体型，而非个体特异的皮肤图案，导致识别不准确。
zh.method: 将图像修复作为辅助任务，比较四种编码器骨干网络，结合分类、聚类指标和GradCAM可视化评估。
zh.result: 图像修复辅助任务提升了编码器对皮肤图案结构的敏感性，聚类指标和可视化结果验证了有效性。
zh.help_to_user: 提供了一种增强模型关注皮肤图案的辅助训练方法，适用于图案化动物的个体识别。

idea_spark.idea: 将图像修复任务与对比学习结合，进一步强化嵌入的判别性。
idea_spark.risk: 修复任务可能引入噪声，需谨慎设计掩码策略以避免过拟合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用自监督辅助任务引导模型关注关键特征，可推广至其他细粒度识别问题。

en.tldr: Exploring the clustering capability of inpainting model embeddings for pattern-based individual identification in zebrafish.
en.motivation: Existing models for animal individual identification often focus on background or body shape rather than individually specific skin patterns, leading to poor accuracy.
en.method: Using image inpainting as an auxiliary task, comparing four encoder backbones with classification metrics, clustering metrics, and GradCAM visualizations.
en.result: The inpainting auxiliary task improves encoder sensitivity to skin pattern structure, validated by clustering metrics and visualizations.
en.help_to_user: Provides an auxiliary training method to enhance model focus on skin patterns for individual identification of patterned animals.

idea_spark.idea: Combine inpainting with contrastive learning to further strengthen embedding discriminability.
idea_spark.risk: The inpainting task may introduce noise; careful mask design is needed to avoid overfitting.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using self-supervised auxiliary tasks to guide models toward key features can be generalized to other fine-grained recognition problems.

papers.title: Segmenting proto-halos with vision transformers
papers.summary: The formation of dark-matter halos from small cosmological perturbations generated in the early universe is a highly non-linear process typically modeled through N-body simulations. In this work, we explore the use of deep learning to segment and classify proto-halo regions in the initial density field according to their final halo mass at redshift z=0. We compare two architectures: a fully convolutional neural network (CNN) based on the V-Net design and a U-Net transformer. We find that the transformer-based network significantly outperforms the CNN across all metrics, achieving sub-percent error in the total segmented mass per halo class. Both networks deliver much higher accuracy than the perturbation-theory-based model \textsc{pinocchio}, especially at low halo masses and in the detailed reconstruction of proto-halo boundaries. We also investigate the impact of different input features by training models on the density field, the tidal shear, and their combination. Finally, we use Grad-CAM to generate class-activation heatmaps for the CNN, providing preliminary yet suggestive insights into how the network exploits the input fields.

ai.tldr: 使用视觉Transformer分割初始密度场中的原初暗物质晕区域，并预测其最终质量。
ai.motivation: 传统N体模拟计算成本高，需要更高效的方法从初始条件预测暗物质晕的形成。
ai.method: 比较V-Net CNN和U-Net Transformer两种深度学习架构，输入密度场、潮汐剪切及其组合，并用Grad-CAM解释CNN。
ai.result: Transformer网络在所有指标上显著优于CNN，总分割质量误差低于1%，且优于微扰理论模型PINOCCHIO。
ai.help_to_user: 为宇宙学模拟提供一种快速、高精度的原初晕分割和分类方法。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多尺度Transformer和物理先验（如拉格朗日扰动理论）改进边界重建。
idea_spark.risk: Transformer对高分辨率三维场计算成本高，可能限制大规模应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: Grad-CAM热图显示网络关注潮汐剪切特征，启发设计更高效的物理引导注意力机制。

zh.tldr: 使用视觉Transformer分割初始密度场中的原初暗物质晕区域，并预测其最终质量。

zh.method: 比较V-Net CNN和U-Net Transformer两种深度学习架构，输入密度场、潮汐剪切及其组合，并用Grad-CAM解释CNN。
zh.result: Transformer网络在所有指标上显著优于CNN，总分割质量误差低于1%，且优于微扰理论模型PINOCCHIO。
zh.help_to_user: 为宇宙学模拟提供一种快速、高精度的原初晕分割和分类方法。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多尺度Transformer和物理先验（如拉格朗日扰动理论）改进边界重建。
idea_spark.risk: Transformer对高分辨率三维场计算成本高，可能限制大规模应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: Grad-CAM热图显示网络关注潮汐剪切特征，启发设计更高效的物理引导注意力机制。

en.tldr: Segment proto-halo regions in the initial density field using vision transformers and predict their final mass.
en.motivation: Traditional N-body simulations are computationally expensive; need efficient methods to predict dark matter halo formation from initial conditions.
en.method: Compare V-Net CNN and U-Net Transformer architectures, input density field, tidal shear, and their combination; use Grad-CAM to interpret CNN.
en.result: Transformer significantly outperforms CNN with sub-percent error in total segmented mass per class, and surpasses perturbation-theory model PINOCCHIO.
en.help_to_user: Provides a fast, accurate method for proto-halo segmentation and classification in cosmological simulations.

idea_spark.idea: Combine multi-scale transformers with physical priors (e.g., Lagrangian perturbation theory) to improve boundary reconstruction.
idea_spark.risk: High computational cost of transformers on 3D fields may limit large-scale application.
idea_spark.inspiration: Grad-CAM heatmaps show the network focuses on tidal shear features, inspiring design of more physically guided attention mechanisms.

papers.title: Chiral interactions of fermions and massive gauge fields in Kaluza-Klein models
papers.summary: In Kaluza-Klein theory, gauge fields on $M_4$ arise as components of a higher-dimensional metric defined on $M_4 \times K$. The traditional expectation is that all the gauge fields of the Standard Model are linked to exact Killing vector fields on the internal space. This paper questions that assumption and investigates the properties of 4D gauge fields linked to non-Killing fields on $K$. It is shown that they have massive yet arbitrarily light bosons; they can mix fermions with different masses; and they can have asymmetric couplings to left- and right-handed fermions. None of these properties is easily satisfied by gauge fields linked to internal isometries. So the massive gauge fields produced in this manner circumvent traditional no-go arguments and offer a geometric source of chiral interactions with fermions. This may help to model the weak force within the Kaluza-Klein framework. Technically, the paper uses the language of spin geometry and Riemannian submersions. It studies the higher-dimensional Dirac operator with non-trivial background metrics. The results are derived for a general $K$. They are illustrated explicitly in the simpler cases where $K$ is the two-sphere and the two-torus.

ai.tldr: 本文质疑传统Kaluza-Klein理论中规范场必须来自内空间等距的假设，证明与非Killing场关联的4D规范场可具有任意轻的质量、混合不同质量费米子、并产生左右手不对称耦合，从而规避了传统无-go论证，为在Kaluza-Klein框架内建模弱相互作用提供了几何来源。
ai.motivation: 传统Kaluza-Klein理论中，标准模型规范场被认为与内空间的精确Killing矢量场对应，但这一假设限制了规范场的性质。本文旨在探索与非Killing场关联的规范场是否可能具有更丰富的现象，特别是手性相互作用，从而为弱力的几何起源提供新途径。
ai.method: 使用旋量几何和黎曼浸没的语言，研究具有非平凡背景度规的高维Dirac算子。在一般内空间K上推导结果，并在二维球面和二维环面上进行显式说明。
ai.result: 与非Killing场关联的4D规范场具有以下性质：可产生任意轻的玻色子；可混合不同质量的费米子；可具有左右手费米子的不对称耦合。这些性质是传统内空间等距规范场所不具备的。
ai.help_to_user: 本文为高能物理理论研究者提供了Kaluza-Klein框架下弱相互作用几何建模的新思路，特别是如何规避传统无-go论证。

idea_spark.idea: 利用非Killing矢量场构造具有手性耦合的规范场，可能为弱力的几何起源提供新模型。
idea_spark.risk: 非Killing场对应的规范场质量可能依赖于内空间几何细节，需要具体模型验证其与标准模型的一致性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从旋量几何和黎曼浸没的角度重新审视Kaluza-Klein理论，可能发现更多超越传统等距假设的新物理。

en.tldr: This paper questions the traditional assumption that gauge fields in Kaluza-Klein theory must arise from exact Killing vector fields on the internal space, and shows that 4D gauge fields associated with non-Killing fields can have arbitrarily light masses, mix fermions of different masses, and produce asymmetric couplings to left- and right-handed fermions, thereby circumventing traditional no-go arguments and offering a geometric source of chiral interactions for modeling the weak force.
en.motivation: In Kaluza-Klein theory, Standard Model gauge fields are traditionally linked to exact Killing vector fields on the internal space, which restricts their properties. This paper explores whether gauge fields associated with non-Killing fields can exhibit richer phenomena, especially chiral interactions, to provide a new geometric origin for the weak force.
en.method: Using the language of spin geometry and Riemannian submersions, the higher-dimensional Dirac operator with non-trivial background metrics is studied. Results are derived for a general internal space K and illustrated explicitly for the two-sphere and two-torus.
en.result: 4D gauge fields associated with non-Killing fields can have arbitrarily light bosons, mix fermions of different masses, and have asymmetric couplings to left- and right-handed fermions. These properties are not easily satisfied by gauge fields linked to internal isometries.
en.help_to_user: This paper provides high-energy theorists with a new approach to geometrically model the weak force within the Kaluza-Klein framework, bypassing traditional no-go arguments.

idea_spark.idea: Construct gauge fields with chiral couplings using non-Killing vector fields, potentially offering a new geometric model for the weak force.
idea_spark.risk: The masses of non-Killing gauge fields may depend on detailed internal geometry, requiring specific models to verify consistency with the Standard Model.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting Kaluza-Klein theory from the perspective of spin geometry and Riemannian submersions may reveal new physics beyond the traditional isometry assumption.

papers.title: Topology-Constrained Quantized nnUNet for Efficient and Anatomically Accurate 3D Tooth Segmentation
papers.summary: We propose a topology-constrained quantized nnUNet framework for efficient and anatomically accurate 3D tooth segmentation, addressing the challenges of spatial distortion introduced by quantization in deep learning models. The proposed method integrates a novel tooth-specific topological loss into quantization-aware training, preserving critical anatomical structures such as tooth count, adjacency relationships, and cavity integrity while maintaining computational efficiency. The system employs an 8-bit quantized nnUNet backbone, where weights and activations are dynamically calibrated to minimize precision loss during inference. Furthermore, the topological loss combines connected-component analysis, adjacency consistency, and hole detection penalties, ensuring anatomical fidelity without modifying the underlying network architecture. The joint optimization objective harmonizes cross-entropy loss, quantization regularization, and topological constraints, enabling end-to-end training with gradient approximations for persistent homology terms. Experiments demonstrate that our approach significantly reduces topological errors compared to conventional quantized models, achieving clinically plausible segmentations on dental CBCT scans. The method retains the hardware efficiency of integer-only inference, making it suitable for deployment in resource-constrained clinical environments. This work bridges the gap between computational efficiency and anatomical precision in medical image segmentation, offering a practical solution for real-world dental applications.

ai.tldr: 提出拓扑约束的量化nnUNet框架，在保持计算效率的同时实现解剖学准确的3D牙齿分割。
ai.motivation: 深度学习模型量化引入空间畸变，导致牙齿分割中解剖结构（如牙齿数量、邻接关系、空腔完整性）失真。
ai.method: 在量化感知训练中集成牙齿特异性拓扑损失，结合连通分量分析、邻接一致性和空洞检测惩罚，使用8位量化nnUNet骨干网络，联合优化交叉熵损失、量化正则化和拓扑约束。
ai.result: 相比传统量化模型显著减少拓扑错误，在CBCT扫描上生成临床合理的分割结果，支持纯整数推理。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种在资源受限临床环境中部署高效且解剖精确的牙齿分割模型的方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将拓扑损失与混合精度训练结合，或探索动态量化策略以进一步减少精度损失。
idea_spark.risk: 拓扑损失可能增加训练复杂度，且对噪声数据敏感；量化后模型泛化性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用拓扑数据分析（如持续同调）作为正则化项，提升分割的解剖合理性。

en.tldr: Proposes a topology-constrained quantized nnUNet framework for efficient and anatomically accurate 3D tooth segmentation.
en.motivation: Quantization in deep learning models introduces spatial distortion, compromising anatomical structures (tooth count, adjacency, cavity integrity) in tooth segmentation.
en.method: Integrates a tooth-specific topological loss into quantization-aware training, combining connected-component analysis, adjacency consistency, and hole detection penalties, using an 8-bit quantized nnUNet backbone with joint optimization of cross-entropy loss, quantization regularization, and topological constraints.
en.result: Significantly reduces topological errors compared to conventional quantized models, producing clinically plausible segmentations on CBCT scans while retaining integer-only inference efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Provides a method to deploy efficient and anatomically accurate tooth segmentation models in resource-constrained clinical settings.

idea_spark.idea: Combine topological loss with mixed-precision training or explore dynamic quantization strategies to further reduce precision loss.
idea_spark.risk: Topological loss may increase training complexity and be sensitive to noisy data; generalization of quantized models needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use topological data analysis (e.g., persistent homology) as a regularizer to enhance anatomical plausibility in segmentation.

papers.title: Reward-Guided Semantic Evolution for Test-time Adaptive Object Detection
papers.summary: Open-vocabulary object detection with vision-language models (VLMs) such as Grounding DINO suffers from performance degradation under test-time distribution shifts, primarily due to semantic misalignment between text embeddings and shifted visual embeddings of region proposals. While recent test-time adaptive object detection methods for VLM-based either rely on costly backpropagation or bypass semantic misalignment via external memory, none directly and efficiently align text and vision in a training-free manner. To address this, we propose Reward-Guided Semantic Evolution (RGSE), a training-free framework that directly refines the text embeddings at test time. Inspired by evolutionary search, RGSE treats text embedding adaptation as a semantic search process: it perturbs text embeddings as candidate variants, evaluates them via cosine similarity with current and historical high-confidence visual proposals as a reward signal, and fuses them into a refined embedding through reward-weighted averaging. Without any backpropagation, RGSE achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple detection benchmarks while adding minimal computational overhead. Our code will be open source upon publication.

ai.tldr: 提出奖励引导语义进化（RGSE），一种无需训练、无需反向传播的测试时自适应框架，通过进化搜索直接优化文本嵌入，以对齐视觉-语言模型在分布偏移下的语义。
ai.motivation: 开放词汇目标检测在测试时分布偏移下性能下降，现有方法要么依赖昂贵的反向传播，要么绕过语义对齐，缺乏高效且无需训练的直接对齐方案。
ai.method: 将文本嵌入适应视为语义搜索过程：扰动文本嵌入生成候选变体，用当前和历史高置信度视觉提议的余弦相似度作为奖励信号，通过奖励加权平均融合得到精炼嵌入。
ai.result: 在多个检测基准上达到最先进性能，且计算开销极小。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种轻量级、即插即用的测试时自适应方法，无需重新训练即可提升开放词汇检测器在分布偏移场景下的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 将文本嵌入的适应视为黑箱优化问题，利用历史高置信度预测作为奖励信号，避免梯度计算。
idea_spark.risk: 奖励信号依赖当前和历史预测质量，若初始预测偏差大可能导致错误累积；进化搜索的收敛性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受进化算法启发，将测试时适应转化为语义空间中的搜索与融合，无需模型微调或额外存储。

en.tldr: Proposes Reward-Guided Semantic Evolution (RGSE), a training-free, backpropagation-free test-time adaptation framework that directly refines text embeddings via evolutionary search to align semantics under distribution shifts for vision-language models.
en.motivation: Open-vocabulary object detection suffers performance degradation under test-time distribution shifts due to semantic misalignment; existing methods rely on costly backpropagation or bypass alignment, lacking efficient training-free direct alignment.
en.method: Treats text embedding adaptation as a semantic search process: perturbs text embeddings as candidate variants, evaluates them via cosine similarity with current and historical high-confidence visual proposals as reward signal, and fuses them via reward-weighted averaging.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple detection benchmarks with minimal computational overhead.
en.help_to_user: Provides a lightweight, plug-and-play test-time adaptation method that improves robustness of open-vocabulary detectors under distribution shifts without retraining.

idea_spark.idea: Treats text embedding adaptation as a black-box optimization problem, using historical high-confidence predictions as reward signals to avoid gradient computation.
idea_spark.risk: Reward signal depends on current and historical prediction quality; poor initial predictions may cause error accumulation; convergence of evolutionary search needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by evolutionary algorithms, formulates test-time adaptation as search and fusion in semantic space without model fine-tuning or extra storage.

papers.title: Anatomy of a failure: When, how, and why deep vision fails in scientific domains
papers.summary: Mirroring its ubiquity in popular media and all human activities, the use of deep learning (DL) is rapidly growing in scientific imaging modalities. However, unlike everyday RGB pictures, pixels encode precise physicochemical properties in scientific imaging across potentially thousands of channels. While DL is well validated on human-centric RGB perceptual tasks, its effectiveness for scientific imaging remains uncertain. Here, we show that the naive application of DL frameworks to scientific images can lead to critical failures. We evaluate the use of DL for pathology, comparing RGB images of stained tissue with the quantitative and information-rich biochemical signatures of infrared (IR) imaging. Despite this informational advantage, DL models trained on IR data paradoxically underperform. We investigate this discrepancy to find that IR data priors interact poorly with the simplicity bias of DL, causing models to collapse to one-dimensional predictions. This constitutes a catastrophic DL failure because the model's representational capacity remains largely unused, while furthermore raising AI safety concerns and undermining the advantages of such scientific modalities. Notably, this problem persists even with state-of-the-art DL robustification strategies, which are primarily designed and validated for RGB imagery and thus inherit the same prior-bias mismatch. This work establishes a framework for understanding the limitations of generic DL in science and advocates for the study of modality-specific failure modes to guide the development of specialized, safe AI algorithms.

ai.tldr: 本研究揭示了深度学习在科学成像中的失败机制：当应用于红外成像等科学模态时，由于数据先验与深度学习简单性偏好的不匹配，模型会崩溃为一维预测，即使使用最先进的鲁棒化策略也无法解决。
ai.motivation: 深度学习在科学成像中广泛应用，但其在非RGB科学模态下的有效性尚未得到充分验证，存在潜在的失败风险。
ai.method: 通过对比病理学中RGB染色组织图像与红外成像的定量生化信号，训练深度学习模型并分析其性能差异。
ai.result: 红外数据训练的模型性能反而更差，原因是数据先验与深度学习简单性偏好冲突，导致模型仅使用一维预测，浪费了表征能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户识别在科学成像中直接套用通用深度学习框架的风险，并理解失败的根本原因。

idea_spark.idea: 开发针对科学成像模态的专用先验编码方法，例如在损失函数中引入物理约束或设计模态特定的网络架构。
idea_spark.risk: 专用方法可能增加计算复杂度，且需要领域专家深度参与，通用性可能降低。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从深度学习在RGB任务中的成功经验出发，但必须重新审视其假设在科学数据中的适用性，推动模态感知的AI算法设计。

en.tldr: This study reveals a failure mechanism of deep learning in scientific imaging: when applied to modalities like infrared imaging, a mismatch between data priors and DL's simplicity bias causes models to collapse to one-dimensional predictions, persisting even with state-of-the-art robustification strategies.
en.motivation: Deep learning is widely used in scientific imaging, but its effectiveness for non-RGB scientific modalities remains uncertain, posing potential failure risks.
en.method: By comparing RGB pathology images with quantitative infrared imaging data, deep learning models were trained and their performance discrepancies analyzed.
en.result: Models trained on infrared data paradoxically underperformed due to a prior-bias mismatch, leading to one-dimensional predictions and underutilized representational capacity.
en.help_to_user: Helps users identify risks of naively applying generic deep learning frameworks to scientific imaging and understand the root causes of failure.

idea_spark.idea: Develop modality-specific prior encoding methods, such as incorporating physical constraints into loss functions or designing specialized network architectures.
idea_spark.risk: Specialized methods may increase computational complexity and require deep domain expertise, potentially reducing generalizability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Learn from DL's success in RGB tasks but critically re-examine its assumptions for scientific data, driving the design of modality-aware AI algorithms.

keywords_raw: deep learning failure in scientific imaging

papers.title: RemoteZero: Geospatial Reasoning with Zero Human Annotations
papers.summary: Geospatial reasoning requires models to resolve complex spatial semantics and user intent into precise target locations for Earth observation. Recent progress has liberated the reasoning path from manual curation, allowing models to generate their own inference chains. Yet a final dependency remains: they are still supervised by human-annotated ground-truth coordinates. This leaves the reasoning process autonomous, but not its spatial endpoint, and prevents true self-evolution on abundant unlabeled remote sensing data. To break this bottleneck, we introduce RemoteZero, a box-supervision-free framework for geospatial reasoning. RemoteZero is motivated by a simple asymmetry: an MLLM is typically better at verifying whether a region satisfies a query than at directly generating precise coordinates. Leveraging this stronger discriminative ability, RemoteZero replaces geometric supervision with intrinsic semantic verification and enables GRPO training without box annotations. The resulting framework further supports iterative self-evolution, allowing the model to improve from unlabeled remote sensing imagery through its own verification signal. Experiments show that RemoteZero achieves competitive performance against strong supervised methods, demonstrating the potential of self-verifying training for geospatial reasoning localization.

ai.tldr: RemoteZero 提出了一种无需人工标注坐标的遥感地理空间推理框架，利用多模态大模型的判别能力替代几何监督，实现自验证训练。
ai.motivation: 现有地理空间推理方法虽然推理路径可自动生成，但最终定位仍需人工标注坐标，阻碍了模型在无标注遥感数据上的自我进化。
ai.method: 利用多模态大模型在验证区域是否符合查询方面优于直接生成坐标的判别能力，以语义验证替代几何监督，并采用 GRPO 训练实现无框监督。
ai.result: 在多个基准上达到与强监督方法相当的性能，展示了自验证训练在地理空间定位中的潜力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不依赖人工标注的情况下，通过模型自身的验证信号实现遥感图像中目标位置的推理与定位。

idea_spark.idea: 利用大语言模型的判别能力替代生成能力，将定位问题转化为验证问题，从而摆脱对精确坐标标注的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 模型判别能力可能在某些复杂场景下不足，导致验证信号噪声大，影响训练稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发研究者思考如何利用模型自身更强的能力（如判别）来辅助较弱的能力（如生成），实现自监督学习。

en.tldr: RemoteZero proposes a geospatial reasoning framework that eliminates the need for human-annotated coordinates by leveraging the discriminative ability of MLLMs to replace geometric supervision with semantic verification.
en.motivation: Existing geospatial reasoning methods still rely on human-annotated coordinates for final localization, preventing self-evolution on abundant unlabeled remote sensing data.
en.method: Exploits the asymmetry that MLLMs are better at verifying whether a region satisfies a query than generating precise coordinates, replacing geometric supervision with semantic verification and enabling GRPO training without box annotations.
en.result: Achieves competitive performance against strong supervised methods, demonstrating the potential of self-verifying training for geospatial localization.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to perform geospatial reasoning and localization without human annotations, using the model's own verification signals.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage the discriminative ability of large language models to replace generative ability, transforming localization into a verification problem to eliminate dependence on precise coordinate annotations.
idea_spark.risk: The model's discriminative ability may be insufficient in complex scenarios, leading to noisy verification signals and training instability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires researchers to think about using stronger abilities of the model (e.g., discrimination) to assist weaker abilities (e.g., generation) for self-supervised learning.

papers.title: Hierarchical Visual Agent: Managing Contexts in Joint Image-Text Space for Advanced Chart Reasoning
papers.summary: Advanced chart question answering requires both precise perception of small visual elements and multi-step reasoning across several subplots. While existing MLLMs are strong at understanding single plots, they often struggle with multi-step reasoning across multiple subplots. We propose HierVA, a hierarchical visual agent framework for chart reasoning that iteratively constructs and updates a working context in a joint image--text space. A high-level manager generates plans and maintains a compact context containing only key information, while specialized workers perform reasoning, gather evidence, and return results. In particular, the agent maintains separate visual and textual contexts, using a zoom-in tool to restrict the visual context. Experiments on the CharXiv reasoning subset demonstrate consistent improvements over strong multimodal baselines, and ablation studies verify that hierarchical architecture, scoped visual context, and distilled context contribute complementary gains.

ai.tldr: 提出层次化视觉代理框架HierVA，通过联合图像-文本空间中的工作上下文管理，提升图表多步推理能力。
ai.motivation: 现有MLLM在单图理解上表现良好，但在多子图跨步推理中常因上下文管理不足而失败。
ai.method: 高层管理器生成计划并维护紧凑上下文，专业工人执行推理、收集证据；通过缩放工具限制视觉上下文，分离视觉与文本上下文。
ai.result: 在CharXiv推理子集上持续优于强多模态基线，消融实验验证层次架构、限定视觉上下文和蒸馏上下文的互补增益。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过层次化代理和上下文管理提升复杂图表问答的准确性和推理能力。

idea_spark.idea: 结合动态缩放和蒸馏上下文，可进一步探索自适应上下文长度控制。
idea_spark.risk: 层次化架构可能增加推理延迟，且对复杂场景的泛化性需更多验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类逐步聚焦关键信息的认知过程启发，将视觉与文本上下文分离管理。

en.tldr: Proposes HierVA, a hierarchical visual agent framework that manages working context in joint image-text space to improve multi-step chart reasoning.
en.motivation: Existing MLLMs excel at single-plot understanding but struggle with multi-step reasoning across multiple subplots due to poor context management.
en.method: A high-level manager generates plans and maintains a compact context; specialized workers perform reasoning and gather evidence; a zoom-in tool restricts visual context, with separate visual and textual contexts.
en.result: Consistent improvements over strong multimodal baselines on the CharXiv reasoning subset; ablation studies confirm complementary gains from hierarchical architecture, scoped visual context, and distilled context.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how hierarchical agents and context management improve accuracy and reasoning in complex chart question answering.

idea_spark.idea: Combining dynamic zooming and distilled context could enable adaptive context length control.
idea_spark.risk: Hierarchical architecture may increase inference latency, and generalization to more complex scenarios requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human cognitive processes of progressively focusing on key information, separating visual and textual context management.

papers.title: InterMesh: Explicit Interaction-Aware End-to-End Multi-Person Human Mesh Recovery
papers.summary: Humans constantly interact with their surroundings. Existing end-to-end multi-person human mesh recovery methods, typically based on the DETR framework, capture inter-human relationships through self-attention across all human queries. However, these approaches model interactions only implicitly and lack explicit reasoning about how humans interact with objects and with each other. In this paper, we propose InterMesh, a simple yet effective framework that explicitly incorporates human-environment interaction information into human mesh recovery pipeline. By leveraging a human-object interaction detector, InterMesh enriches query representations with structured interaction semantics, enabling more accurate pose and shape estimation. We design lightweight modules, Contextual Interaction Encoder and Interaction-Guided Refiner, to integrate these features into existing HMR architectures with minimal overhead. We validate our approach through extensive experiments on 3DPW, MuPoTS, CMU Panoptic, Hi4D, and CHI3D datasets, demonstrating remarkable improvements over state-of-the-art methods. Notably, InterMesh reduces MPJPE by 9.9% on CMU Panoptic and 8.2% on Hi4D, highlighting its effectiveness in scenarios with complex human-object and inter-human interactions.

ai.tldr: 提出InterMesh，一种显式融入人-物交互信息的多人体网格恢复框架，在多个数据集上显著提升精度。
ai.motivation: 现有端到端多人体网格恢复方法仅通过自注意力隐式建模交互，缺乏对人-物、人-人交互的显式推理。
ai.method: 利用人-物交互检测器提取结构化交互语义，设计轻量级模块（上下文交互编码器和交互引导精炼器）将其融入现有HMR架构。
ai.result: 在CMU Panoptic和Hi4D数据集上MPJPE分别降低9.9%和8.2%，超越现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种简单有效的交互感知框架，可直接增强现有HMR模型对复杂交互场景的建模能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将交互检测器输出的结构化关系（如接触点、交互类型）作为显式先验，引导人体姿态和形状的联合优化。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖交互检测器的质量，在未见交互类型或遮挡严重时可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴场景图或关系网络的思想，将交互关系显式编码为图结构，增强模型对多人-多物场景的推理能力。

en.tldr: Proposes InterMesh, a framework that explicitly incorporates human-object interaction information into multi-person human mesh recovery, achieving significant improvements on multiple datasets.
en.motivation: Existing end-to-end multi-person HMR methods model interactions only implicitly via self-attention, lacking explicit reasoning about human-object and inter-human interactions.
en.method: Leverages a human-object interaction detector to extract structured interaction semantics, and designs lightweight modules (Contextual Interaction Encoder and Interaction-Guided Refiner) to integrate them into existing HMR architectures.
en.result: Reduces MPJPE by 9.9% on CMU Panoptic and 8.2% on Hi4D, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides a simple yet effective interaction-aware framework that can directly enhance existing HMR models for complex interaction scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Use structured relations (e.g., contact points, interaction types) from an interaction detector as explicit priors to guide joint optimization of human pose and shape.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the quality of the interaction detector; may introduce noise for unseen interaction types or heavy occlusions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow ideas from scene graphs or relational networks to explicitly encode interactions as graph structures, enhancing reasoning in multi-person multi-object scenes.

papers.title: Detecting Hallucinations in Large Language Models via Internal Attention Divergence Signals
papers.summary: We propose a lightweight and single-pass uncertainty quantification method for detecting hallucinations in Large Language Models. The method uses attention matrices to estimate uncertainty without requiring repeated sampling or external models. Specifically, we measure the Kullback-Leibler divergence between each attention head's distribution and a uniform reference distribution, and use these features in a logistic regression probe. Across multiple datasets, task types, and model families, attention divergence is highly predictive of answer correctness and performs competitively with existing uncertainty estimation methods. We find that this signal is concentrated in middle layers and on factual tokens such as named entities and numbers, suggesting that attention dynamics provides an efficient and interpretable white-box signal of model uncertainty.

ai.tldr: 提出一种轻量级、单次前向传播的不确定性量化方法，通过注意力头分布与均匀参考分布的KL散度检测大语言模型幻觉。
ai.motivation: 现有幻觉检测方法需多次采样或外部模型，计算成本高且缺乏可解释性。
ai.method: 计算每个注意力头分布与均匀分布的KL散度，作为特征输入逻辑回归探针，预测回答正确性。
ai.result: 在多个数据集、任务类型和模型家族上，注意力散度信号高度预测回答正确性，性能与现有方法相当，且集中于中间层和事实性token。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、可解释的幻觉检测工具，无需额外采样或模型，适用于实时推理场景。

idea_spark.idea: 结合注意力散度与token级置信度，构建多信号融合的幻觉检测框架。
idea_spark.risk: 信号可能对特定任务或数据分布敏感，需验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用注意力分布偏离均匀分布的程度作为模型不确定性的内在指标，启发设计更通用的不确定性量化方法。

idea_spark.inspiration: 利用注意力分布偏离均匀分布的程度作为模型不确定性的内在指标，启发设计更通用的不确定性量化方法。

en.tldr: A lightweight, single-pass uncertainty quantification method detects LLM hallucinations by measuring KL divergence between attention head distributions and a uniform reference.
en.motivation: Existing hallucination detection methods require repeated sampling or external models, incurring high computational cost and lacking interpretability.
en.method: Compute KL divergence of each attention head's distribution from a uniform distribution, then use these features in a logistic regression probe to predict answer correctness.
en.result: Across multiple datasets, task types, and model families, attention divergence is highly predictive of answer correctness, competitive with existing methods, and concentrated in middle layers and factual tokens.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient, interpretable hallucination detection tool without extra sampling or models, suitable for real-time inference.

idea_spark.idea: Combine attention divergence with token-level confidence to build a multi-signal fusion framework for hallucination detection.
idea_spark.risk: Signal may be sensitive to specific tasks or data distributions; generalization needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using deviation of attention distribution from uniform as an intrinsic uncertainty indicator inspires design of more general uncertainty quantification methods.

papers.title: Private Structured-Subset Retrieval
papers.summary: We introduce the \emph{Private Structured-Subset Retrieval (PSSR)} problem, where a user retrieves $D$ messages from a database of $K$ messages replicated across $N$ non-colluding servers, and the demand is restricted to a known structured family of $D$-subsets. This formulation generalizes classical Private Information Retrieval (PIR) and multi-message PIR (MPIR), and captures settings where the demand space is constrained by application-specific structure. Focusing on balanced ${\{0,1\}}$-linear schemes, we derive converse bounds on the maximum retrieval rate and minimum subpacketization level, and develop an optimization-based framework for constructing schemes for general structured demand families. Our results show that, for certain families, the PSSR rate converse bound can exceed the best-known MPIR rate upper bound; when this PSSR bound is achievable, MPIR rate-optimal schemes become suboptimal for those families. By exploiting demand structure, our PSSR schemes achieve higher retrieval rates for many families and never underperform the best-known balanced ${\{0,1\}}$-linear MPIR schemes. Our results also show that demand structure can reduce the required subpacketization even when the optimal rate is unchanged. Our parallel work on contiguous-demand families further illustrates the scope of this framework by yielding rate-optimal schemes with substantially smaller subpacketization and no field-size restrictions, improving upon MPIR-based schemes.

ai.tldr: 提出私有结构化子集检索问题，通过利用需求结构在检索速率和子包化水平上优于传统多消息PIR方案。
ai.motivation: 传统PIR和MPIR未考虑应用中的需求结构，导致速率受限且子包化水平高。
ai.method: 定义PSSR问题，聚焦平衡{0,1}-线性方案，推导速率上界和子包化下界，并构建基于优化的通用方案框架。
ai.result: 对某些需求族，PSSR速率上界超过已知MPIR速率上界；PSSR方案可达到更高速率，且从不低于最优MPIR方案；需求结构可降低子包化水平。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何利用需求结构提升隐私检索效率，为设计更优的分布式存储检索协议提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 将PSSR框架应用于联邦学习中的梯度聚合，利用梯度结构（如稀疏性）降低通信开销。
idea_spark.risk: 需求结构可能被攻击者利用，需额外保护结构隐私。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受经典PIR与MPIR的局限性启发，思考如何通过问题结构突破已知性能界限。

idea_spark.risk: 需求结构可能被攻击者利用，需额外保护结构隐私。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受经典PIR与MPIR的局限性启发，思考如何通过问题结构突破已知性能界限。

en.tldr: Introduces Private Structured-Subset Retrieval, which exploits demand structure to achieve higher retrieval rates and lower subpacketization than traditional multi-message PIR schemes.
en.motivation: Classical PIR and MPIR ignore application-specific demand structure, leading to suboptimal rates and high subpacketization.
en.method: Formulates PSSR, focuses on balanced {0,1}-linear schemes, derives converse bounds on rate and subpacketization, and develops an optimization-based framework for general structured demand families.
en.result: For certain families, the PSSR rate bound exceeds the best-known MPIR bound; PSSR schemes achieve higher rates and never underperform optimal MPIR schemes; demand structure can reduce subpacketization even when optimal rate is unchanged.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to leverage demand structure to improve privacy retrieval efficiency, providing theoretical guidance for designing better distributed storage retrieval protocols.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the PSSR framework to gradient aggregation in federated learning, leveraging gradient structure (e.g., sparsity) to reduce communication overhead.
idea_spark.risk: Demand structure may be exploited by adversaries, requiring additional protection of structural privacy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the limitations of classical PIR and MPIR, thinking about how to break known performance bounds by exploiting problem structure.

papers.title: LineRides: Line-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Bicycle Robot Stunts
papers.summary: Designing reward functions for agile robotic maneuvers in reinforcement learning remains difficult, and demonstration-based approaches often require reference motions that are unavailable for novel platforms or extreme stunts. We present LineRides, a line-guided learning framework that enables a custom bicycle robot to acquire diverse, commandable stunt behaviors from a user-provided spatial guideline and sparse key-orientations, without demonstrations or explicit timing. LineRides handles physically infeasible guidelines using a tracking margin that permits controlled deviation, resolves temporal ambiguity by measuring progress via traveled distance along the guideline, and disambiguates motion details through position- and sequence-based key-orientations. We evaluate LineRides on the Ultra Mobility Vehicle (UMV) and show that the policy trained with our methods supports seamless transitions between normal driving and stunt execution, enabling five distinct stunts on command: MiniHop, LargeHop, ThreePointTurn, Backflip, and DriftTurn.

ai.tldr: 提出LineRides框架，通过用户提供的空间引导线和稀疏关键朝向，无需演示即可让自行车机器人学会多种特技动作。
ai.motivation: 强化学习中敏捷机器人动作的奖励函数设计困难，且演示方法对新型平台或极端特技缺乏参考动作。
ai.method: 使用空间引导线定义路径，引入跟踪裕度处理不可行引导线，通过沿引导线行进距离解决时间模糊性，利用位置和序列关键朝向消除运动细节歧义。
ai.result: 在Ultra Mobility Vehicle上训练的策略支持正常驾驶与特技执行的无缝切换，实现五种可命令特技：MiniHop、LargeHop、ThreePointTurn、Backflip和DriftTurn。
ai.help_to_user: 为机器人敏捷动作学习提供一种无需演示、仅需简单引导线的奖励设计方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将引导线概念扩展到三维空间，结合动态障碍物避免，实现实时特技规划。
idea_spark.risk: 引导线设计可能过于简化，导致在高度动态或非结构化环境中策略泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类通过路径标记学习复杂动作的启发，将抽象空间线索转化为机器人奖励信号。

en.tldr: LineRides is a line-guided learning framework that enables a bicycle robot to acquire diverse stunt behaviors from a user-provided spatial guideline and sparse key-orientations without demonstrations.
en.motivation: Designing reward functions for agile robotic maneuvers in RL is difficult, and demonstration-based approaches lack reference motions for novel platforms or extreme stunts.
en.method: It uses a spatial guideline with a tracking margin for infeasible paths, resolves temporal ambiguity via distance-based progress, and disambiguates motion details using position- and sequence-based key-orientations.
en.result: The policy trained on the Ultra Mobility Vehicle supports seamless transitions between normal driving and five commandable stunts: MiniHop, LargeHop, ThreePointTurn, Backflip, and DriftTurn.
en.help_to_user: Provides a demonstration-free reward design method for agile robot learning using simple spatial guidelines.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the guideline concept to 3D space with dynamic obstacle avoidance for real-time stunt planning.
idea_spark.risk: Guideline design may oversimplify, leading to poor policy generalization in highly dynamic or unstructured environments.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by how humans learn complex maneuvers using path markers, translating abstract spatial cues into robot reward signals.

papers.title: Local Intrinsic Dimension Unveils Hallucinations in Diffusion Models
papers.summary: Diffusion models are prone to generating structural hallucinations - samples that match the statistical properties of the training data yet defy underlying structural rules, resulting in anomalies like hands with more than five fingers. Recent research studied this failure mode from several viewpoints, offering partial explanations to their occurrence, such as mode interpolation. In this work, we propose a complementary perspective that treats hallucinations as instabilities on the model-induced manifold. We begin by showing that a hallucination filter based on such instabilities matches or exceeds the performance of the recently proposed temporal one. By tracing the source of these instabilities, we identify local intrinsic dimension (LID) as their primary driver and propose Intrinsic Quenching (IQ), a direct corrective mechanism that deflates it to alleviate hallucinations. IQ consistently outperforms standard hallucination reduction baselines across a wide array of benchmarks and offers a highly promising solution for enforcing anatomical consistency in downstream medical imaging tasks.

ai.tldr: 提出局部内在维度（LID）作为扩散模型结构幻觉的驱动因素，并设计内在淬灭（IQ）机制来缓解幻觉。
ai.motivation: 扩散模型常生成符合统计分布但违反结构规则的幻觉（如多指手），现有解释不完整，需要新视角。
ai.method: 将幻觉视为模型诱导流形上的不稳定性，通过局部内在维度（LID）量化不稳定性，并设计内在淬灭（IQ）直接降低LID以抑制幻觉。
ai.result: 基于LID的幻觉过滤器性能优于近期提出的时间过滤器；IQ在多个基准上持续优于标准幻觉减少基线，并在医学成像中有效保证解剖一致性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解扩散模型幻觉的几何根源，并提供一种无需额外训练即可减少幻觉的实用方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将LID作为训练过程中的正则化项，从源头抑制模型学习高维流形上的不稳定区域。
idea_spark.risk: LID计算可能增加推理开销；过度降低LID可能限制模型生成多样性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从流形几何视角重新审视生成模型的失败模式，启发基于拓扑或几何的鲁棒生成方法。

zh.tldr: 提出局部内在维度（LID）作为扩散模型结构幻觉的驱动因素，并设计内在淬灭（IQ）机制来缓解幻觉。
zh.motivation: 扩散模型常生成符合统计分布但违反结构规则的幻觉（如多指手），现有解释不完整，需要新视角。
