idea_spark.inspiration: 利用显式几何先验引导注意力机制，减少对大量标注数据的依赖。

en.tldr: Proposes Angle-I2P, an outlier rejection network using angle-consistent geometric constraints and hierarchical attention to improve image-to-point-cloud registration under low inlier ratio.
en.motivation: Existing deep learning methods struggle with accurate PnP results when initial matching inlier ratio is low.
en.method: Designs a scale-invariant cross-modality geometric constraint based on angular consistency, and a global-to-local hierarchical attention mechanism to filter geometrically inconsistent matches.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on 7Scenes, RGBD Scenes V2, and a self-collected dataset, with consistent improvements in inlier ratio and registration recall.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how geometric constraints and attention can enhance robustness in cross-modal registration.

idea_spark.idea: Combining hierarchical attention with geometric constraints for global filtering then local refinement can generalize to multi-modal feature fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Generalization under extreme viewpoint changes or heavy occlusion needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using explicit geometric priors to guide attention reduces reliance on large annotated datasets.

papers.title: Geometry-Aware State Space Model: A New Paradigm for Whole-Slide Image Representation
papers.summary: Accurate analysis of histopathological images is critical for disease diagnosis and treatment planning. Whole-slide images (WSIs), which digitize tissue specimens at gigapixel resolution, are fundamental to this process but require aggregating thousands of patches for slide-level predictions. Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) tackles this challenge with a two-stage paradigm, decoupling tile-level embedding and slide-level prediction. However, most existing methods implicitly embed patch representations in homogeneous Euclidean spaces, overlooking the hierarchical organization and regional heterogeneity of pathological tissues. This limits current models' ability to capture global tissue architecture and fine-grained cellular morphology. To address this limitation, we introduce a hybrid hyperbolic-Euclidean representation that embeds WSI features in dual geometric spaces, enabling complementary modeling of hierarchical tissue structures and local morphological details. Building on this formulation, we develop BatMIL, a WSI classification framework that leverages both geometric spaces. To model long-range dependencies among thousands of patches, we employ a structured state space sequence model (S4) backbone that encodes patch sequences with linear computational complexity. Furthermore, to account for regional heterogeneity, we introduce a chunk-level mixture-of-experts (MoE) module that groups patches into regions and dynamically routes them to specialized subnetworks, improving representational capacity while reducing redundant computation. Extensive experiments on seven WSI datasets spanning six cancer types demonstrate that BatMIL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art MIL approaches in slide-level classification tasks. These results indicate that geometry-aware representation learning offers a promising direction for next-generation computational pathology.

ai.tldr: 提出几何感知状态空间模型BatMIL，通过双曲-欧几里得混合表示和状态空间序列模型，实现全切片图像的高效分类。
ai.motivation: 现有方法将病理切片特征嵌入同质欧几里得空间，忽略了组织层次结构和区域异质性，限制了全局架构和细胞形态的捕捉能力。
ai.method: 采用双曲-欧几里得混合表示建模层次结构与局部细节；以结构化状态空间序列模型（S4）为骨干编码补丁序列；引入分块混合专家模块动态路由区域特征。
ai.result: 在七个涵盖六种癌症类型的WSI数据集上，BatMIL持续优于现有MIL方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种兼顾全局组织架构与局部形态的病理图像分析新范式，可提升诊断准确性。

idea_spark.idea: 将双曲空间嵌入与状态空间模型结合，用于处理具有层次结构的长序列数据。
idea_spark.risk: 双曲空间计算复杂度较高，且混合表示可能引入额外的超参数调优挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受组织病理学层次结构启发，利用双曲空间自然编码树状结构，结合S4高效处理长序列。

idea_spark.risk: 双曲空间计算复杂度较高，且混合表示可能引入额外的超参数调优挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受组织病理学层次结构启发，利用双曲空间自然编码树状结构，结合S4高效处理长序列。

en.tldr: Proposes BatMIL, a geometry-aware state space model using hybrid hyperbolic-Euclidean representation and S4 backbone for whole-slide image classification.
en.motivation: Existing methods embed patches in homogeneous Euclidean space, ignoring hierarchical tissue organization and regional heterogeneity, limiting capture of global architecture and fine-grained morphology.
en.method: Uses hybrid hyperbolic-Euclidean representation to model hierarchical structure and local details; employs structured state space sequence model (S4) as backbone; introduces chunk-level mixture-of-experts for dynamic routing.
en.result: BatMIL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art MIL methods on seven WSI datasets across six cancer types.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new paradigm for computational pathology that balances global tissue architecture and local morphology, improving diagnostic accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Combine hyperbolic embedding with state space models for long sequential data with hierarchical structures.
idea_spark.risk: Hyperbolic space computation is complex, and hybrid representation may introduce additional hyperparameter tuning challenges.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by hierarchical organization in histopathology, hyperbolic space naturally encodes tree-like structures, while S4 efficiently handles long sequences.

papers.title: Quantum-Resistant Networks: A Review of Primitives, Protocols and Best Practices
papers.summary: Large-scale quantum computers threaten the public-key cryptographic foundations underpinning today's network security infrastructures. While significant progress has been made in standardizing post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) primitives and adapting individual protocols such as TLS and SSH, far less attention has been paid to the broader architectural consequences of the post-quantum transition for networked systems. In particular, many real-world deployments such as mobile networks, industrial control systems, IoT environments, and regulated infrastructures cannot assume the universal availability, deployability, or desirability of PQ public-key infrastructures. This paper presents the first comprehensive systematization of PQ-resistant network architectures, focusing on key distribution and management as a system-level design problem rather than a protocol-local substitution. We introduce a unified taxonomy spanning cryptographic foundations (symmetric-only, PQ-PKI, hybrid, and information-theoretic multi-path), key-distribution architectures (centralized, hierarchical, replicated, threshold, MPC-backed, and serverless), trust and threat models, key-management lifecycle, and deployment environments. Using this framework, we analyze the security, scalability, and operational trade-offs of a wide range of architectures under realistic PQ adversary assumptions, including harvest-now, decrypt-later attacks and partial infrastructure compromise. Our study highlights fundamental gaps in existing approaches, clarifies when PQ-PKI is necessary or avoidable, and identifies promising research directions for building cryptographically agile, quantum-resilient network infrastructures.

ai.tldr: 本文首次系统化梳理了抗量子网络架构，聚焦密钥分发与管理作为系统级设计问题，提出了涵盖密码学基础、密钥分发架构、信任模型等的统一分类法，并分析了实际部署中的安全与可扩展性权衡。
ai.motivation: 大规模量子计算机威胁现有公钥密码体系，但现有研究多关注密码原语和单个协议适配，缺乏对网络系统整体架构转型的全面审视。
ai.method: 通过构建统一分类法（密码学基础、密钥分发架构、信任与威胁模型、密钥生命周期、部署环境），系统分析多种架构在抗量子攻击者假设下的安全、可扩展性和操作权衡。
ai.result: 揭示了现有方法的根本性差距，阐明了何时需要或可避免PQ-PKI，并指出了构建密码学敏捷、量子弹性网络基础设施的研究方向。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解抗量子网络转型中的架构选择、权衡和关键研究空白，指导实际部署决策。

idea_spark.idea: 探索基于信息论多路径密钥分发与对称密码结合的无PKI抗量子网络架构。
idea_spark.risk: 信息论方法可能面临高延迟或带宽开销，且缺乏标准化支持。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“harvest-now, decrypt-later”攻击出发，设计混合密钥生命周期管理策略，结合短期对称密钥与长期PQ密钥轮换。

en.tldr: This paper presents the first comprehensive systematization of quantum-resistant network architectures, focusing on key distribution and management as a system-level design problem, and proposes a unified taxonomy covering cryptographic foundations, key-distribution architectures, trust models, and deployment environments.
en.motivation: Large-scale quantum computers threaten current public-key cryptography, but existing work focuses on primitives and individual protocol adaptation, lacking a holistic view of network architecture transition.
en.method: A unified taxonomy (cryptographic foundations, key-distribution architectures, trust/threat models, key lifecycle, deployment environments) is used to systematically analyze security, scalability, and operational trade-offs under realistic quantum adversary assumptions.
en.result: Identifies fundamental gaps in existing approaches, clarifies when PQ-PKI is necessary or avoidable, and highlights promising research directions for cryptographically agile, quantum-resilient network infrastructures.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand architectural choices, trade-offs, and research gaps in the quantum-resistant network transition, guiding practical deployment decisions.

idea_spark.idea: Explore information-theoretic multi-path key distribution combined with symmetric-only cryptography for PKI-free quantum-resistant network architectures.
idea_spark.risk: Information-theoretic approaches may incur high latency or bandwidth overhead and lack standardization support.
idea_spark.inspiration: From 'harvest-now, decrypt-later' attacks, design hybrid key lifecycle management combining short-term symmetric keys with long-term PQ key rotation.

papers.title: Securing the Web with HSTS-Enforced
papers.summary: TLS stripping attacks expose sensitive web traffic by forcing secure HTTPS connections to fall back to unencrypted HTTP. At present, protection against these attacks relies on website operators explicitly opting into security by deploying mechanisms such as HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) headers. These mechanisms have significant limitations: some are weak or difficult to configure, which raises the risk of misconfiguration and reduces practical adoption; others violate HTTP backward compatibility; at least one can even be abused to enable unintended user tracking.   We introduce HSTS-Enforced, a mechanism that eliminates the remaining attack surface for TLS stripping while still allowing operators to securely specify that their websites need to be accessed over HTTP when necessary, thereby maintaining accessibility. To achieve this, we flip the current opt-in security model to an opt-out model: all connections default to HTTPS, and operators can explicitly opt out if their websites require HTTP using so-called HTTP-Required indicators. We propose two such HTTP-Required indicators: a new DNS record and an HTTP-Required Preload list. We evaluate HSTS-Enforced under multiple deployment scenarios, demonstrating that it blocks all practical TLS stripping attempts while maintaining compatibility for sites that require HTTP - without introducing overhead in the typical case. Finally, we outline a practical transition path to accelerate global adoption.

ai.tldr: 提出HSTS-Enforced机制，将当前HSTS的主动选择安全模型改为默认HTTPS的主动退出模型，以消除TLS剥离攻击面，同时保持对需要HTTP的网站的兼容性。
ai.motivation: 现有HSTS等机制存在配置困难、向后兼容性差、可能被滥用于追踪用户等问题，且依赖网站运营者主动启用，导致实际部署不足，无法有效防御TLS剥离攻击。
ai.method: 通过翻转安全模型为默认HTTPS，引入两种HTTP-Required指示器（新DNS记录和HTTP-Required预加载列表），允许运营者明确声明需要HTTP访问的网站。
ai.result: 在多种部署场景下评估表明，HSTS-Enforced能阻止所有实际TLS剥离尝试，同时保持对需要HTTP的网站的兼容性，且典型情况下不引入额外开销。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解一种新的、更安全的Web传输安全机制，以及它如何在不牺牲兼容性的前提下防御TLS剥离攻击。

idea_spark.idea: 利用DNS记录作为安全指示器，将安全策略从应用层下沉到网络基础设施层，可能简化部署并增强可靠性。
idea_spark.risk: 新DNS记录需要全球DNS系统支持，部署初期可能面临兼容性和缓存问题；预加载列表的维护和更新可能滞后。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“选择加入”到“选择退出”的范式转换，为设计更安全的默认系统提供了思路。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解一种新的、更安全的Web传输安全机制，以及它如何在不牺牲兼容性的前提下防御TLS剥离攻击。

idea_spark.idea: 利用DNS记录作为安全指示器，将安全策略从应用层下沉到网络基础设施层，可能简化部署并增强可靠性。
idea_spark.risk: 新DNS记录需要全球DNS系统支持，部署初期可能面临兼容性和缓存问题；预加载列表的维护和更新可能滞后。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“选择加入”到“选择退出”的范式转换，为设计更安全的默认系统提供了思路。

en.tldr: Proposes HSTS-Enforced, which flips the current opt-in security model to an opt-out model where all connections default to HTTPS, eliminating TLS stripping attack surface while maintaining compatibility for sites that require HTTP.
en.motivation: Existing mechanisms like HSTS rely on website operators opting in, suffer from misconfiguration, backward compatibility issues, and potential abuse for tracking, leading to insufficient deployment against TLS stripping.
en.method: By defaulting to HTTPS and introducing two HTTP-Required indicators (a new DNS record and an HTTP-Required Preload list), operators can explicitly opt out for sites that need HTTP.
en.result: Evaluation under multiple deployment scenarios shows HSTS-Enforced blocks all practical TLS stripping attempts, maintains compatibility for HTTP-required sites, and introduces no overhead in the typical case.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand a new, more secure web transport security mechanism that defends against TLS stripping without sacrificing compatibility.

idea_spark.idea: Using DNS records as security indicators moves security policy from the application layer to the network infrastructure layer, potentially simplifying deployment and enhancing reliability.
idea_spark.risk: New DNS records require global DNS system support; early deployment may face compatibility and caching issues; preload list maintenance may lag.
idea_spark.inspiration: The paradigm shift from opt-in to opt-out provides a blueprint for designing more secure default systems.

papers.title: AISSA: Implementation and Deployment of an AI-based Student Slides Analysis tool for Academic Presentations
papers.summary: Providing timely and actionable feedback on oral presentation slides is challenging in higher education, particularly in large classes where teachers cannot realistically deliver detailed formative feedback before students present. This paper introduces AISSA (AI-based Student Slides Analysis tool), a web-based system that combines large language models (LLMs) and Learning Analytics dashboards to support scalable, rubric-based feedback on presentation slides. AISSA allows students to upload their slide decks prior to an oral presentation and automatically receive quantitative scores and qualitative feedback based on teacher-defined evaluation rubrics. The system analyzes both slide-level features and slide content, generates structured feedback through an LLM (ChatGPT 5.2), and presents the results through interactive dashboards for students and teachers. We tested AISSA on a pilot deployment with 46 undergraduate students in a real academic setting. The results indicate that AISSA is technically reliable, economically feasible, and perceived by students as useful for iterative slide improvement. These findings suggest that combining LLM-based analysis with Learning Analytics dashboards is a promising approach for supporting formative feedback on presentation slides at scale.

ai.tldr: 提出并部署了一个基于AI的学生幻灯片分析工具AISSA，结合大语言模型和学习分析仪表盘，为学术演示提供可扩展的、基于量规的形成性反馈。
ai.motivation: 高等教育中，教师难以在大型课堂上为学生的演示幻灯片提供及时、可操作的形成性反馈。
ai.method: 构建Web系统AISSA，学生上传幻灯片后，系统分析幻灯片层面特征和内容，通过ChatGPT 5.2生成定量评分和定性反馈，并通过交互式仪表盘展示结果。
ai.result: 在46名本科生的真实场景中测试，系统技术可靠、经济可行，学生认为有助于迭代改进幻灯片。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助教师自动评估学生演示幻灯片，提供结构化反馈，减轻教师负担，同时帮助学生提前改进演示材料。

idea_spark.idea: 扩展系统支持实时演示反馈，或集成语音分析以评估口头表达。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM可能产生不准确或偏见的反馈，需人工审核；学生可能过度依赖自动反馈而忽视教师指导。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM的语义理解能力，结合量规自动评分，为大规模教育评估提供新思路。

idea_spark.idea: 扩展系统支持实时演示反馈，或集成语音分析以评估口头表达。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM可能产生不准确或偏见的反馈，需人工审核；学生可能过度依赖自动反馈而忽视教师指导。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM的语义理解能力，结合量规自动评分，为大规模教育评估提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes and deploys AISSA, an AI-based tool combining LLMs and Learning Analytics dashboards to provide scalable, rubric-based formative feedback on academic presentation slides.
en.motivation: In higher education, teachers cannot deliver timely, actionable formative feedback on presentation slides in large classes.
en.method: Builds a web system where students upload slides; the system analyzes slide-level features and content, generates quantitative scores and qualitative feedback via ChatGPT 5.2, and presents results through interactive dashboards.
en.result: Pilot with 46 undergraduates shows the system is technically reliable, economically feasible, and perceived as useful for iterative slide improvement.
en.help_to_user: Helps teachers automatically assess student presentation slides with structured feedback, reducing workload, and helps students improve materials before presenting.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the system to support real-time presentation feedback or integrate speech analysis for oral delivery evaluation.
idea_spark.risk: LLM dependency may produce inaccurate or biased feedback requiring human oversight; students might over-rely on automated feedback and neglect teacher guidance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging LLM semantic understanding with rubric-based scoring offers a new paradigm for large-scale educational assessment.

papers.title: ipc_shared_ptr: A Publish/Subscribe-Aware Smart Pointer for Cross-Process Object Lifetime Management
papers.summary: True zero-copy Inter-Process Communication (IPC) in publish/subscribe (pub/sub) middleware such as Robot Operating System 2 (ROS 2) requires subscribers to reference message objects in publisher-owned shared memory. Objects must not be reclaimed while referenced, yet must eventually be reclaimed, with correct handling of crash recovery and Transient Local QoS retention requirements. We propose ipc_shared_ptr, a pub/sub-aware smart pointer for cross-process message lifetime management. ipc_shared_ptr exploits pub/sub structural properties to specialize Birrell's reference listing, limiting global metadata updates to per-subscriber 0<->1 transitions and achieving an order-of-magnitude reduction in global communication over general-purpose distributed reference counting. We analyze the key metadata management tradeoff: scalability versus implementation simplicity. Owner-driven reclaim offers greater scalability, but concurrent membership changes and reclamation decisions produce races that widen the correctness-verification state space. Single-writer achieves structural atomicity, eliminating this complexity at the cost of a centralized bottleneck. iceoryx2 (owner-driven reclaim) and Agnocast -- a true zero-copy ROS 2 IPC middleware sharing the publisher's heap with subscribers and adopting ipc_shared_ptr with single-writer -- embody each architecture. Comparative evaluation at the scale of Autoware -- the largest open-source ROS 2 application -- confirms that single-writer achieves sufficient scalability: at 200 topics, two subscribers per topic and 100 Hz, Agnocast's E2E p99.9 is 2.9x lower than iceoryx2's, justifying implementation simplicity over owner-driven reclaim.

ai.tldr: 提出一种面向发布/订阅模式的智能指针 ipc_shared_ptr，用于跨进程对象生命周期管理，在 ROS 2 中实现真正的零拷贝 IPC。
ai.motivation: 在发布/订阅中间件（如 ROS 2）中，订阅者需要引用发布者共享内存中的消息对象，但对象既不能在引用期间被回收，又必须最终被回收，同时要正确处理崩溃恢复和 Transient Local QoS 保留需求。
ai.method: 利用发布/订阅结构特性特化 Birrell 的引用列表，将全局元数据更新限制为每个订阅者的 0<->1 转换，大幅减少全局通信；并分析了 owner-driven reclaim 与 single-writer 两种架构的权衡。
ai.result: 在 Autoware 规模评估中，采用 single-writer 的 Agnocast 在 200 个主题、每主题 2 个订阅者、100 Hz 下，E2E p99.9 比 iceoryx2 低 2.9 倍，证明 single-writer 的扩展性足以满足需求。
ai.help_to_user: 为 ROS 2 等发布/订阅系统提供一种高效、安全的跨进程对象生命周期管理方案，简化实现并提升性能。

idea_spark.idea: 利用发布/订阅的结构特性（如订阅者数量有限、生命周期可预测）来优化分布式引用计数，减少全局同步开销。
idea_spark.risk: single-writer 架构可能成为中心化瓶颈，在极端高负载下扩展性受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从 Birrell 的引用列表出发，结合领域特定知识进行特化，是提升分布式系统性能的有效思路。

zh.tldr: 提出一种面向发布/订阅模式的智能指针 ipc_shared_ptr，用于跨进程对象生命周期管理，在 ROS 2 中实现真正的零拷贝 IPC。
zh.motivation: 在发布/订阅中间件（如 ROS 2）中，订阅者需要引用发布者共享内存中的消息对象，但对象既不能在引用期间被回收，又必须最终被回收，同时要正确处理崩溃恢复和 Transient Local QoS 保留需求。

zh.result: 在 Autoware 规模评估中，采用 single-writer 的 Agnocast 在 200 个主题、每主题 2 个订阅者、100 Hz 下，E2E p99.9 比 iceoryx2 低 2.9 倍，证明 single-writer 的扩展性足以满足需求。
zh.help_to_user: 为 ROS 2 等发布/订阅系统提供一种高效、安全的跨进程对象生命周期管理方案，简化实现并提升性能。

idea_spark.idea: 利用发布/订阅的结构特性（如订阅者数量有限、生命周期可预测）来优化分布式引用计数，减少全局同步开销。
idea_spark.risk: single-writer 架构可能成为中心化瓶颈，在极端高负载下扩展性受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从 Birrell 的引用列表出发，结合领域特定知识进行特化，是提升分布式系统性能的有效思路。

en.tldr: Proposes ipc_shared_ptr, a publish/subscribe-aware smart pointer for cross-process object lifetime management, enabling true zero-copy IPC in ROS 2.
en.motivation: In pub/sub middleware like ROS 2, subscribers need to reference message objects in publisher-owned shared memory; objects must not be reclaimed while referenced but must eventually be reclaimed, with correct handling of crash recovery and Transient Local QoS retention.
en.method: Exploits pub/sub structural properties to specialize Birrell's reference listing, limiting global metadata updates to per-subscriber 0<->1 transitions, achieving an order-of-magnitude reduction in global communication; analyzes tradeoff between owner-driven reclaim and single-writer architectures.
en.result: Evaluation at Autoware scale shows Agnocast (single-writer) achieves 2.9x lower E2E p99.9 than iceoryx2 (owner-driven reclaim) at 200 topics, 2 subscribers per topic, 100 Hz, justifying implementation simplicity.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and safe cross-process object lifetime management solution for pub/sub systems like ROS 2, simplifying implementation and improving performance.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage pub/sub structural properties (e.g., limited subscribers, predictable lifetimes) to optimize distributed reference counting and reduce global synchronization overhead.
idea_spark.risk: Single-writer architecture may become a centralized bottleneck under extreme high load, limiting scalability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Specializing Birrell's reference listing with domain-specific knowledge is an effective approach to improve distributed system performance.

papers.title: Patterns of Developer Adoption of LLM-Generated Code Refactoring Suggestions
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) have gained widespread popularity and have steadily improved over time, enabling software developers to use them for various code-related tasks. One common task is code refactoring, where the LLM suggests changes for the developer to apply to their code to improve quality attributes such as readability or maintainability. While current research focuses on evaluating LLM-generated refactoring suggestions, there is a limited understanding of how developers apply these suggestions in practice. To explore this, we analyze 169 GitHub commits where developers refactor their code based on a ChatGPT conversation linked in the commit message. We found that developers mostly accept and use the suggestions without modifications. When changes are made, they are mostly major and fall into five different patterns that depend on the refactoring activity, the developer's prompt, and the validity of the response from ChatGPT.

ai.tldr: 分析169个GitHub提交，发现开发者大多直接接受LLM生成的代码重构建议，修改时多为重大修改，且存在五种模式。
ai.motivation: 现有研究侧重评估LLM重构建议的质量，但缺乏对开发者实际采纳行为的理解。
ai.method: 收集并分析169个GitHub提交，这些提交的提交信息中链接了ChatGPT对话，开发者据此重构代码。
ai.result: 开发者通常直接采纳建议；修改时多为重大修改，且模式取决于重构活动、提示词和ChatGPT响应的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解开发者如何实际使用LLM重构建议，指导工具设计和培训。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个插件，实时分析开发者对LLM建议的修改模式，并自适应调整建议风格。
idea_spark.risk: 可能过度依赖LLM，削弱开发者重构能力；建议需平衡自动化与学习。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从开发者修改模式中提炼最佳实践，用于改进LLM提示工程和输出质量。

zh.tldr: 分析169个GitHub提交，发现开发者大多直接接受LLM生成的代码重构建议，修改时多为重大修改，且存在五种模式。
zh.motivation: 现有研究侧重评估LLM重构建议的质量，但缺乏对开发者实际采纳行为的理解。

idea_spark.risk: 可能过度依赖LLM，削弱开发者重构能力；建议需平衡自动化与学习。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从开发者修改模式中提炼最佳实践，用于改进LLM提示工程和输出质量。

en.tldr: Analyzing 169 GitHub commits, we found that developers mostly accept LLM-generated code refactoring suggestions without modifications; when changes occur, they are mostly major and follow five patterns.
en.motivation: Current research evaluates LLM refactoring suggestions but lacks understanding of how developers actually apply them in practice.
en.method: We analyzed 169 GitHub commits where developers refactored code based on a ChatGPT conversation linked in the commit message.
en.result: Developers mostly accept suggestions as-is; modifications are mostly major and depend on refactoring activity, prompt, and response validity.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into real-world adoption of LLM refactoring suggestions, informing tool design and developer training.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a plugin that analyzes developer modification patterns in real time and adapts suggestion style accordingly.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on LLM may weaken developer refactoring skills; balance automation with learning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extract best practices from developer modification patterns to improve LLM prompt engineering and output quality.

papers.title: An Evaluation of Chat Safety Moderations in Roblox
papers.summary: Roblox is among the most popular online gaming platforms, used by hundreds of millions of users every day. A substantial portion of these users are underage, who are at a greater risk, where abusive users may utilize Roblox's real-time chat interface to make the initial contact with potential victims. Roblox employs automated chat moderation mechanisms to detect potentially abusive messages; however, to date, their effectiveness has not been independently investigated. Toward this goal, we collected approximately 2 million chat messages from four games across multiple age groups and analyzed them to evaluate the moderation system. These messages were collected from public game servers following ethical and legal norms as well as Roblox's terms of service.   We use this corpus to qualitatively study which types of unsafe chats escape the moderation system and how policy-violating users evade the moderation system. Given the dataset's scale, it is prohibitively expensive to conduct qualitative content analysis manually. Therefore, we adopt a two-step approach. First, we manually labeled safe and unsafe messages (n=99.8K) and used them as a ground truth to evaluate four locally hosted state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs). Next, the best-performing LLM was applied to the entire corpus to identify potentially unsafe messages, which we manually categorized using iterative open and axial coding methods until thematic saturation was reached. Overall, our findings reveal a troublesome reality: numerous instances of unsafe chat messages related to grooming, sexualizing minors, bullying, & harassment, violence, self-harm, and sharing sensitive information, etc., escaped the current moderation. Our analysis of users whose messages were previously flagged revealed that they continue to send harmful messages by employing a wide range of techniques to evade the moderation system.

ai.tldr: 评估了Roblox聊天审核系统，发现大量涉及诱骗、性化未成年人、欺凌等不安全消息未被拦截，且违规用户使用多种规避手段。
ai.motivation: Roblox拥有数亿用户，其中大量为未成年人，但其聊天审核系统的有效性尚未被独立研究。
ai.method: 收集约200万条聊天消息，人工标注9.98万条作为基准，评估四个本地大语言模型，用最佳模型标注全部消息，再通过迭代编码进行主题分类。
ai.result: 当前审核系统未能有效拦截诱骗、性化、欺凌、暴力、自残等不安全消息，违规用户采用多种规避技术继续发送有害内容。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解Roblox聊天审核的漏洞，提高家长和平台对未成年人保护的认识。

idea_spark.idea: 结合大语言模型与人工编码，高效识别大规模聊天数据中的不安全内容。
idea_spark.risk: 自动标注可能误判或漏判，需谨慎设计基准和编码过程。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发开发更智能的实时审核系统，针对规避技术进行动态检测。

zh.tldr: 评估了Roblox聊天审核系统，发现大量涉及诱骗、性化未成年人、欺凌等不安全消息未被拦截，且违规用户使用多种规避手段。
zh.motivation: Roblox拥有数亿用户，其中大量为未成年人，但其聊天审核系统的有效性尚未被独立研究。
zh.method: 收集约200万条聊天消息，人工标注9.98万条作为基准，评估四个本地大语言模型，用最佳模型标注全部消息，再通过迭代编码进行主题分类。
zh.result: 当前审核系统未能有效拦截诱骗、性化、欺凌、暴力、自残等不安全消息，违规用户采用多种规避技术继续发送有害内容。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解Roblox聊天审核的漏洞，提高家长和平台对未成年人保护的认识。

idea_spark.idea: 结合大语言模型与人工编码，高效识别大规模聊天数据中的不安全内容。
idea_spark.risk: 自动标注可能误判或漏判，需谨慎设计基准和编码过程。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发开发更智能的实时审核系统，针对规避技术进行动态检测。

en.tldr: Evaluated Roblox chat moderation and found numerous unsafe messages (grooming, sexualization, bullying, etc.) escaping detection, with violators using various evasion techniques.
en.motivation: Roblox has hundreds of millions of users, many underage, yet its chat moderation effectiveness had not been independently studied.
en.method: Collected ~2 million chat messages, manually labeled 99.8K as ground truth, evaluated four local LLMs, applied the best model to the full corpus, and categorized unsafe messages via iterative coding.
en.result: Current moderation fails to block many unsafe messages related to grooming, sexualization, bullying, violence, self-harm, etc.; violators employ diverse evasion techniques.
en.help_to_user: Highlights gaps in Roblox chat safety, aiding parents and platforms in better protecting minors.

idea_spark.idea: Combine LLMs with human coding to efficiently identify unsafe content in large-scale chat data.
idea_spark.risk: Automated labeling may have errors; careful ground truth and coding design are essential.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires development of smarter real-time moderation systems that dynamically detect evasion tactics.
